DEDICATION
 To, friends and the people who dream of writing a
book.
 Restless Nights
 Education system of India (promoting me to realize
how much of a misfit I am)
 Memories of People Came and Gone
 Finally, to parents who taught me how to dream
and woke me up when I overslept. But always
supported even my wildest of endeavours.
This book shall forever mark the existence of
people gone, started as a practical Joke this
book holds a weird but exciting tale of
imagination but what it offers is a way to
reignite the flames of Life for those who lost
them.
i
Threshold’s Pursuit
1. The Tower’s Edge
(Jerry’s POV)
As I stood atop the metallic tower, the overcast sky
mirrored the heaviness in my heart. It seemed that only a
select few possessed the power to truly change the world,
while the rest of us remained nameless and powerless,
striving for a mere semblance of peace before our
inevitable demise. Most people seemed content with the
way things were, but there were a few who yearned for
something more, and even fewer who strived for the
greater good. However, the foundation of capitalism,
deeply embedded in the evolution of humanity, had set us
on a path where the rotting core of greed prevented us
from progressing far.
I had no intentions of witnessing the consequences of
this decay; I wouldn’t be around to see it unfold. In my
belief, the concept of victory was the harbinger of doom
for our world. Victory could not exist without defeat, and
with defeat came restless souls. These restless souls
became obsessed with the pursuit of victory, leading them
astray from the path of humanity and the principles we, as
humans, had collectively decided upon as laws.
Whenever I encountered the concept of victory, I found
myself looking for the failures instead. The victors were
always celebrated and comforted, but it was the losers
who truly needed support. Yet, I couldn’t expect society
to hold loser parades or acknowledge their struggle. It had
become commonplace for us to associate victory with a
sense of pride.
1
Teerth Sharma
Perhaps it was this distorted understanding of victory
that led humanity to invent destructive weapons like
nuclear bombs. These weapons offered the immediate
satisfaction of victory over Japan, a temptation that
American leaders couldn’t resist. The allure of victory
was and will always be strong, deeply ingrained in our
nature. However, we cannot blame God for the mess we
have created in this world. Humans mostly associated
losing with bad luck, but I would argue that true defeat
only occurs when the person never had a chance to win.
Otherwise, it is nothing but a humiliating blow dealt by
the opposition.
I found myself typing these thoughts on a famous
micro-blogging site, fully aware that they were neither
good news nor particularly relevant. However, I wanted
this message to reach everyone, hoping that someone out
there might change their perspective. I didn’t seek
gratitude for it; all I desired was to contribute to making
this world a better place. As I typed, I reminisced about
my eventful life thus far. I had been fortunate to have
good friends and to be the fun-loving type. However,
recent experiences had opened my eyes to unpleasant
truths, prompting me to sever contact with everyone
except one person—Charlie (Charles).
Charlie was quite the opposite of what people
perceived him to be. Although most would find it
laughable if they heard me describe him as a nice guy, he
always carried an invincible attitude. It seemed like he
had no weaknesses, except perhaps for the scorching sun.
Life had treated him well, and he was a perfect example
of a winner. Maybe that’s why we were such close
friends—opposites attract.
2
Threshold’s Pursuit
“Enough of this sentimental thinking,” I told myself
aloud, trying to gather my resolve. I had come to this
tower with the intention of ending my life, and that was
precisely what I would do. Shouting out, I reassured
myself that I wasn’t the kind of person who acted
impulsively or avoided overthinking. I should have
jumped from this tower hours ago, but I had been
preoccupied with thoughts of that micro-blog. Strangely
enough, I was scared of dying alone, even though
loneliness wasn’t the reason for my suicidal thoughts.
The catalyst for my despair had been my attempt to
propose to the love of my life, only to be met with a
barrage of slander from her and her friends in my class.
They maliciously attacked my character in front of their
boyfriends and besties, blurring the line between the two.
Although using the term “love of my life” at my age might
sound cringy, I genuinely believed that if I could be with
her, I wouldn’t let her go. It was a childish belief, but we
all needed something to hold onto in order to keep living.
These fabricated beliefs had sustained me for the past two
years, but they weren’t perfect or powerful enough to
shield me from the overwhelming temptation of self-
sacrifice. I believed that ending my life would bring peace
of mind to my family, who suffered every day because of
my failures. They made it abundantly clear through their
expressions and words that they no longer wanted me
around, considering me a burden—a depreciating asset.
They say that your life flashes before your eyes in your
final moments, that you see the balance of good and bad
deeds. I wondered what I would see. Would the images be
more painful, or would they bring me peace in my last
moments on this plane of existence? It took immense
3
Teerth Sharma
courage to stand here now. Although reaching the top of
this tower had been my own choice, my brain wasn’t quite
ready to die. Our natural instinct is to cling to life, but I
had reached my breaking point. I refused to be a coward,
as I had been throughout my entire existence.
With determined steps, I moved closer to the edge of
the red metallic beam at the top of the cell tower. Peering
down, I saw the overcast sky enveloping the entire city.
In that moment, I felt a flicker of happiness for Charlie,
who always found joy in the rain, regardless of the
circumstances. As the thought of his contentment brought
me a moment of peace, I silenced the swirling thoughts in
my mind. “Stop thinking, Jerry,” I commanded myself,
and without further hesitation, I leaped into the void.
As I hurtled toward the ground, a scream escaped my
lips, blending with the rushing wind. The acceleration of
9.8 m/s² only amplified the sense of impending doom. The
end was clear to me—it wasn’t a vision of white light or
encounters with divine beings. I knew I was dead because,
after I “opened my eyes” or whatever sensation I could
perceive in this unfamiliar state, all I sensed was darkness.
There was no shock, for I had anticipated this outcome. I
had expected to be trapped forever in the fields of
asphodel, unable to feel control or escape the torment of
existence. That was the very essence of my decision to
end my life—to cease feeling anything at all.
Ironically, it seemed that God hadn’t finished toying
with me yet. Instead of granting me the blissful release of
an unconscious void, He allowed me to retain my
consciousness, leaving me to suffer within my thoughts.
This punishment was far worse than anything the fields of
punishment could offer. Yet, deep down, I understood
4
Threshold’s Pursuit
that it was the right punishment for me. I had willingly
embraced it. Time became a distorted concept, difficult to
measure or comprehend, as the white void engulfed the
plane of existence I found myself in. It was blinding—
pure and all-encompassing.
5
Teerth Sharma

2. Bound By Will.title: 2. Bound By Will
2. Bound By Will.text:
As I emerged from the blinding white light, my senses
were immediately overwhelmed by the sheer magnitude
of the space around me. It stretched out as far as my eyes
could see, an expansive expanse of white that seemed
endless in every direction. But then, something incredible
happened—the space began to rotate, like a massive
graviton coming to life.
At first, the rotation was slow, and I didn’t pay it much
attention, my mind already clouded by the strangeness of
the situation. However, I soon regretted my indifference,
for as soon as I stood up, the graviton activated with
tremendous force, hurling me toward the edge of the
rotating circle at an alarming speed. It was as if the
centrifugal and centripetal forces were locked in a delicate
equilibrium, keeping me pinned to that spot, even though
there appeared to be no physical boundary.
With this revelation in mind, I resisted the urge to get
up and move closer to the center, knowing that the
centrifugal force would only intensify, pushing me back
to where I started. “So this is supposed to be my hell,” I
yelled in frustration, hoping that someone, somewhere,
could hear me. But in that moment, as soon as the word
“hell” left my lips, the rotation abruptly ceased, and a
sudden calm settled over the space.
I stood up, trying to make sense of my surroundings.
Perhaps I was lying in a hospital bed, near the end of my
life, and the anesthesia hadn’t taken effect. That would be
the only logical explanation for the bizarre experience I
6
Threshold’s Pursuit
was going through. “So much for healthcare,” I shouted,
half-jokingly, hoping that the doctors could still hear me.
But before I could dwell on my unfortunate fate, the arena
began to shake violently, as if an earthquake were rocking
the very foundation of the white expanse.
Instinctively, I scrambled to find something to hold
onto, searching for an edge or a wall to steady myself.
Yet, no matter how much I panicked or how hard I looked,
there were no walls within reach. The earthquake-like
tremors made it feel as though the ground beneath me was
breaking apart, creating a sinkhole within the expanse of
white. Still, with trembling knees, I managed to maintain
my balance, my body growing accustomed to the
powerful shaking.
And then, without warning, the graviton sprang back
to life. It spun with such velocity that it would have made
even the most daring amusement park enthusiasts weep
with joy. I was flung even farther, landing on the ground
and now facing the endless expanse above me. As I lay
there, I realized that I had reached a point of equilibrium,
where the opposing forces of centrifugal and centripetal
motion canceled each other out. But the rotation
continued, propelling me in a circular path at speeds that
defied all comprehension.
Nausea churned in my stomach, but when I tried to
vomit, my body refused to cooperate. I felt as if I were
having a heart attack, yet my pulse miraculously returned
to normal. The sensation of imminent fainting washed
over me, but my body refused to succumb, trapped in this
relentless cycle of rotation. The jolts of vibration from the
ground shot up my spine, leaving me feeling utterly
helpless. In that moment, I couldn’t help but berate myself
7
Teerth Sharma
for not studying physics more diligently. If only I were
more adept, perhaps I could calculate an escape velocity
from this nightmarish predicament. But this was a
rotational motion problem of the most challenging kind—
one that not even the most ardent engineering enthusiast
could solve.
The abuse continued relentlessly, stretching on for
what felt like an eternity. Time seemed impossible to
gauge in this vast expanse of white. And then, as suddenly
as it had begun, everything came to a halt. A sense of
warmth spread through me, akin to the feeling I
experienced when I was near someone I cherished. It was
in that moment that a voice, soothing yet authoritative,
resonated within me.
“My child, your fate has caused grief among your
fellow mortals, yet I found enjoyment in the plunge you
took toward the iron tower. However, I am not here to
engage in conversation. I have shifted a mere 15% of my
presence to witness what kind of hell a mortal human
creates after succumbing to the weight of their own
thoughts. Most humans are responsible for crafting their
own hells, drowning in guilt. And those who are not
consumed by guilt create their own heavens, only to
blame us for the path that leads them there.”
With a brief pause, the voice continued, “But I have an
offer for you. Your strong will, even after choosing to end
your own life, has caught my attention. Your conscious
mind may be burdened with guilt, but your subconscious
clings to hope and possesses a strength uncommon among
mortals. I require individuals like you, and thus, I present
you with an offer.”
Curiosity mingled with anticipation, compelling me to
8
Threshold’s Pursuit
listen intently. The voice predicted my question before I
even uttered it, counting down from three. Without
hesitation, I seized the opportunity and blurted out, “Who
are you, and where am I?”
“I am what you mortals call God, but my true name is
חור שדוק,” the voice replied. The words resonated with
such power that I felt as if my eardrums were about to
burst. Dizziness washed over me, and I struggled to regain
my balance. “You are in a reality plane where all departed
souls find themselves. However, unlike other souls, your
plane remains intact due to your unyielding will, even
after taking your own life. That is precisely why I need
you to undertake a task for me.”
The voice conveyed a touch of concern as it continued,
“You see, I created this vast multiverse, interconnected by
bridges that allow me to traverse different realms.
However, the bridge that connects me to my first world
has been damaged and altered, barring my entry. If I were
to attempt crossing over, the bridge would be severed
permanently, forever isolating me from the web of the
multiverse.”
Pausing for a moment, the voice explained, “My offer
to you is this: Become an agent of God, granted powers
by a patron angel. Your mission will be to recover and
repair the damaged bridge, and to eliminate the miscreant
who defiled it. In exchange, at the completion of this
mission, I will allow you to return to your world with a
new body, recreated within the first world.”
Overwhelmed with a mix of awe and humility, I
mustered the courage to speak. “But, my Lord, how can a
lowly mortal like me be of use to you, Your Highness?”
The voice, filled with a calm grace, replied, “Though I
9
Teerth Sharma
cannot predict or alter the future, I can offer you
assistance in a different manner. I will grant you the
ability to summon three individuals from your world, who
will become your allies in the first world.”
I pondered the offer for a brief moment, realizing that
it could be a hallucination or a figment of my imagination.
Yet, the prospect of embarking on such a thrilling and
purposeful journey overwhelmed any doubts. Excitement
radiated from every pore of my being as I responded with
unwavering determination, “Yes, my Lord. Consider it
done.”
10
Threshold’s Pursuit

3. The Enigmatic Angel.title: 3. The Enigmatic Angel
3. The Enigmatic Angel.text:
“I appreciate your enthusiasm in becoming a god’s
agent, kid, but you must understand the world you’re
about to enter overflows with all-penetrating mana. Mana
is a potent energy that mortals and monsters alike can
channel and manipulate to suit their needs. Due to its
nature of being attracted to the life force of an organism,
monsters tend to possess large reserves of mana, while
humans have a lesser amount. However, humans
compensate for this by having better control over their
mana, enabling them to wield advanced forms of
channeling, resulting in awe-inspiring results. For
instance, in my previous reincarnated hero’s case, his
summoned party member annihilated nine hundred
million monsters with a single spell. This hero was
rumored to be the strongest in the entire universe.
Now, I know you must be curious about what
happened to that person and what awaits you after
completing your mission of defeating the antagonist.
Your task will be to repair the world’s bridge and
establish a new breed of aristocrats, just as the last hero
did after eliminating the elemental lesser gods. Once you
accomplish this, you will be able to return.”
Conversing with God was always overwhelming. His
omniscience and omnipotence frightened me more than
any challenge I had ever faced. One wrong move, and he
could erase me with a mere thought.
“Don’t worry, my hero. I am omnipresent, and my
choices are infallible,” he said with a playful tone. “Now,
11
Teerth Sharma
you must meet the angel who will serve as your patron.
He will provide further explanations. I hope you fulfill my
bidding well, and remember, I can erase you in the blink
of an eye.” With those words, the resounding voice faded
away, leaving me in shock. God’s voice was humble, yet
it exuded power that could shatter worlds.
Encountering someone so far beyond my level made
me painfully aware of my own weakness. It was not the
first time I had met someone vastly more powerful than
myself, but this encounter emphasized my insignificance.
I knew I could not hope to compete with God, or even be
considered strong in the vastness of the universe.
Before I could fully comprehend the situation, a new
voice echoed through the white expanse. I turned to see a
captivating man before me—resplendent and powerful.
His features were not just handsome; they were timeless
and perfect, like a divine creation. His copper-glowing
skin and the dazzling blue shades of his eyes left me
mesmerized.
“Brother, I agree that we exist in a reality boundless
by infinite time. But I don’t have all day, so come near to
me,” the angel said in a casual yet deep voice that
resonated within my bones. Eager to comply, I ran
towards him, curiosity and awe overwhelming me.
“You keep calling me brother, but I have never seen
you before,” I said, trying to understand the connection he
claimed we had.
“Obviously, you are my brother now, as we follow the
same cause. You have been directly chosen by my father,
which makes us brothers in arms. I am the archangel of
strength, warriors, and known as the God’s judgment. I
will be your patron,” he explained, his presence radiating
12
Threshold’s Pursuit
power and confidence.
The mention of his angelic powers piqued my interest.
“What powers will I gain from being under God’s
judgment?”
“You will possess enhanced speed, thought
processing, vitality, durability, super healing, angelic
strength, the ability to wield the Weapon-God skill, and
the divine judgment. But remember, dude, chill! If you
weren’t compatible with me, some other angel might have
burned you to the ground for your impatience,” the
archangel responded nonchalantly.
Curiosity sparked within me, and I couldn’t resist
asking, “What makes you so special, sir, and what do you
mean by ‘compatible’?”
He chuckled before answering, “Well, I am the
youngest archangel, and God has deemed me the best and
strongest. Being compatible means that you and I are a
perfect match. Angels are assigned to heroes based on
their personality and life force. You’re lucky to be
compatible with me, an archangel, instead of some minor
angel like the angel of lies or something... maybe an angel
of suicide, who knows?”
Grateful for his support, I confessed, “Sir, I am truly
honored, but I feel I lack enough knowledge about the
new world. Can you enlighten me further?”
He smirked, his smile taking on a devilish edge,
“Where’s the fun in telling you everything? My father
chose you as a hero for a reason, and I want to see what
he saw in you. You’ll have to discover more on your own.
Just remember, you’re about to enter the most challenging
world in the universe, a place with powers rivaling angels
and God.”
13
Teerth Sharma
As if to emphasize his words, the archangel directed
his attention to a particular spot in the white expanse.
Suddenly, the room shifted, and a gate emerged before us.
He warned me that once I entered the gate, I couldn’t
return. “You’re not that lucky, you know. There have
been many reincarnations before you. Your special case
is that you’re going into the hardest world in the
universe—a place with powers that rival angels and God,”
he said mysteriously.
Without warning, he grabbed me by the collar and
threw me into the gate. I felt a mix of fear and excitement
as I crashed into it. The gate opened, and I was whisked
away into a world tinged with blue, leaving behind the
white expanse and the enigmatic archangel.
As I journeyed into the unknown, I couldn’t help but
feel a rush of emotions. Fear and uncertainty mingled with
determination and curiosity, propelling me forward into
the new world where I was to face challenges beyond my
wildest dreams. The power of gods and angels loomed
ahead, and I knew my journey would be extreme, filled
with hardships and triumphs. But deep within, a spark of
hope and purpose burned bright, reminding me that even
in the face of overwhelming power, I had been chosen for
a reason—to be a hero, to mend a broken world, and to
embrace my destiny in a universe brimming with magic
and mystery. Was I ready? ‘God knows’ but I closed my
eyes entering the new world and sharply inhaled.
14
Threshold’s Pursuit

4. The Divine Free Fall.title: 4. The Divine Free Fall
4. The Divine Free Fall.text:
As I opened my eyes, the realization hit me like a
lightning bolt: my archangel had made a grave mistake,
and I was hurtling through the sky at an altitude rivaling
airplanes. The wind roared in my ears, clothes fluttering
wildly, and panic surged within me as I cried out, “Why
am I falling again?” But amidst the fear, a glimmer of
hope emerged. Deep inside, I knew that keeping my wits
about me might just be the key to survival. After all, I had
faced such situations before, and I reminded myself that
composure was my ally – though it hadn’t helped yet, it
was good to keep reminding myself.
As the clouds dispersed, an awe-inspiring sight met
my eyes. Towering trees, colossal in size, stood before
me, some reaching so high that their branches caressed the
clouds like long-lost friends. With determination and
quick thinking, I spotted a chance for survival. “That tree
right there,” I declared aloud, formulating a plan. “I can
grab it to slow my fall and leap to the next one. It’s a slim
chance, but I won’t go down without a fight. After all, it’s
my second chance.” I was desperate not to die this time
around because I wanted to make an impact; last time, I
didn’t leave a mark in the world of my name, but it would
change this time. As I was contemplating this, an ethereal
creature soared gracefully beneath the clouds—a
legendary dragon. Its majestic wings shimmered in the
sunlight, and its iridescent scales mesmerized me. An
audacious idea struck me, and I proclaimed, “I will land
on the dragon!” Adjusting my trajectory, I aimed for the
15
Teerth Sharma
mighty beast. But my daring move didn’t go unnoticed.
The dragon perceived me as a threat, or maybe a snack,
and prepared to devour me.
Yet, in the face of danger, I didn’t plan to stop or
change my trajectory using its mouth as a way. I knew
that would lead to certain death. The only solution was to
follow my original plan. As I reached the dragon’s oral
cavity, I decided that I had to do something about its
massive tongue. I fought with all my might, but it seemed
futile at first. Then, something inside me awakened – my
heart beat raced, and suddenly, I found a newfound
strength. With determination, I pulled with all my might
and ripped its tongue out from its mouth, causing the
dragon to convulse in agony. But my task wasn’t
complete—the dragon could still crush me with its
powerful jaws.
With unwavering determination, I pushed against the
jaws that threatened to crush me. The strength that I had
just displayed vanished as quickly as it came; I knew I had
no chance of winning a battle of strength with a monster
related to alligators and possessing a bite force stronger
than them. I was about to give up when I remembered
what the divine being had said – this world had a magical
power called mana that manifested itself in everyone,
even in me. If my body was tailor-made by a divine being,
it must have an unseen amount of mana. Racing my
thoughts, I focused on the dragon’s jaw and, when it
closed its mouth again, I caught its soft palate, trying to
keep those jaws from crushing me. As I looked inside
myself, I didn’t find anything magical or divine in my
existence – just plain old me in a world where dangers
were more than I could handle. As I was about to be
16
Threshold’s Pursuit
crushed by the jaws’ force, a sudden rush of adrenaline
surged into my stomach, and my muscles felt hot, as if
they were being remade. Then the unimaginable
occurred—the dragon’s jaws yielded, splitting the
creature in two. A cascade of emotions washed over me—
awe, shock, and irritation—but there was no time to feel
anything. I was yet again falling, this time into a
picturesque lake that stretched out before me like an azure
paradise. A moment of serenity amidst chaos. As I
reached the water’s surface, the impact was tremendous.
I closed my eyes, but I noticed that my body managed to
stay in one piece after that impact. I submerged, the force
of the dive carrying me deep into the tranquil embrace of
the lake. I opened my eyes as I neared the bottom of the
lake. There were parts of the dragon there, and something
in that corpse shined, piquing my curiosity. I knew that
dragon’s tooth had many magical uses, chief among them
being the ability to grant courage, and I needed it. I had
been a coward all my life, so I swam deeper towards the
bottom of the lake where the corpse lay, and that’s when
I realized it wasn’t a tooth that glowed so brightly – it was
a claw, as huge as my hand. This claw radiated a pearl-
white glow. It took time for me to notice, but that lake
wasn’t devoid of aquatic life – they were just trying to
avoid me, maybe because I killed this massive creature or
perhaps because they hadn’t seen a human before. They
kept away from me, which I didn’t mind, but I was
amazed by the beauty of the schools of fish of different
colors that matched the palette of the rainbow.
Suddenly, out of nowhere, a shadow hid the sun that
gleamed above the waters of the lake. That shadow was
long and snake-like – a huge sea serpent circling in for the
17
Teerth Sharma
kill. I knew I wasn’t ready to confront another mythical
being in the element they were strongest in, so I grabbed
the glowing claw, used my knees to push myself up to the
surface of the lake at a different location than the serpent.
It didn’t take me long to reach the surface, but I was sure
that my lung capacity wasn’t this huge at any point in my
life. Unfortunately, the sea serpent was still there,
swimming around the surface about 50 feet away to my
left. Then I dived again to confirm that the sea serpent was
long gone. As I looked around underwater, I saw no sign
of the sea serpent, and I thought the problem was long
gone. But I forgot to look directly down, and just as I was
about to swim away to the surface, the sea serpent was
right below me, coming in for the kill. The only option I
had was to swim and dodge him, which is what I started
to do. I swam for my life as the sea serpent followed me,
being right behind me at any given moment. I did all the
strokes I knew, butterfly, freestyle, but then I let my inner
instincts guide me. I dived a bit below the surface for
faster swimming, dodging the sea serpent’s bite by
changing my direction. It was enough, and as I resurfaced,
I saw that land was just within my reach. The sea serpent
jumped out of the water and bit me, but I was suddenly
faced with two choices – keep my arm and die with it, or
leave the area the serpent was biting with him and pull
myself away. Both options were painful, but I didn’t want
to die, and I was sure I couldn’t do anything with my arm,
so I decided to let him pull me deep into the lake.
Suddenly, he left my arm and swam back to whatever
hellhole he came from. I didn’t know why he left me, but
I felt a sinister presence lurking – it was in the same
magnitude as my dear archangel but opposite in nature. I
18
Threshold’s Pursuit
didn’t know what it was, but as I pulled myself to the
shore, I saw my knees give up. When I looked around at
the beautiful expanse, I was on a lake shore, with lush
green plains more vibrant than any green I had seen on
my earth. In front of me were mountains as tall as clouds
and more, touching the clouds as if they were more
dominant, claiming victory. To my left was a deep, deep
redwood jungle, and to my right was a pathway that led to
some sort of clearing in the plains. I needed practice
making decisions because I couldn’t decide what I should
do. The most obvious choice was to take the pathway, but
that would lead me to some kind of civilization, and I
didn’t know how conversation worked here. I thought
about heading into the woods, but then the dangers
lurking there were unknown. So, I was stuck at this
lakeshore until I made up my mind.
19
Teerth Sharma
5. Divine Borrowed Power?
As I sat there, basking in the ethereal warmth of the
sun, I consciously immersed myself in the awe-inspiring
world surrounding me. The realization slowly descended
upon my soul, like a celestial revelation, that my
ambitious quest to win the favor of a deity was an
endeavor far beyond the realm of my imagination. The
power to shape my destiny lay solely in the hands of a
god, yet here I stood, humbly entangled in the eternal
struggle between those who triumphed and those who
faltered.
Undeterred by the enormity of the task, I had
embarked on a selfless endeavor, daring to face and slay
a dragon—a mythical creature undoubtedly more
deserving of life than myself. Contemplating the vastness
of the universe and the inherent limitations that even a god
might face, I found myself questioning the very
boundaries of divine power. Could the celestial beings
mend everything with a mere snap of their fingers, or were
there cosmic rules they, too, had to respect?
As I gracefully rose from my serene spot on the shore,
I gently brushed off the clinging grains of sand that
adorned my clothes. It was at that precise moment that my
vision underwent a wondrous transformation. It felt as
though the very fabric of space had shifted its hue, and as
I held my own hand before me, I noticed an extraordinary
change. My vision now possessed a polarized quality,
with the outline of my hand taking on a striking blue hue.
Every movement appeared to unfold frame by frame, as if
20
Threshold’s Pursuit
my eyes had been blessed with a divine upgrade to a
higher resolution.
Curiosity, like a siren’s call, beckoned me to explore
how this newfound vision would unveil the celestial
canopy above. As I focused on the heavens, they appeared
almost abstract in shape and color. But with diligent
observation, I discerned smaller shapes dancing within
my field of view. These enigmatic forms, though
challenging to decipher, seemed to convey vital
information about my own being—the status of my mana
reserve and health. This knowledge, undeniably useful,
couldn’t entirely suppress the rising anxiety within me.
Knowing the precise extent of my health meant that it
could decrease, potentially leading to pain—a prospect
that filled me with trepidation. Memories of suffering in
my past life haunted me, culminating in the poignant
decision to end it all.
Yet, the fear of pain now drove me to seek a way to
avoid it at all costs. As I hesitated and procrastinated, a
mysterious presence seemed to materialize behind me,
interrupting my thoughts and stirring a mix of dread and
resentment. I turned to face this unexpected visitor, and
there stood a young man, seemingly a stranger from
another realm. He exuded an air of charm and confidence,
his red shirt artfully revealing his chiseled abs, and his
jeans and matching black shoes completing a striking
ensemble. His well-built form, a stark contrast to my own
scrawniness, sparked a tinge of envy, as I had often been
a target for those who wielded their strength over me.
His captivating red irises held my gaze, and while they
seemed a puzzling juxtaposition to his appearance, I paid
little mind to it, for I had resolved to be wary of him—
21
Teerth Sharma
deeming him nothing more than a gym enthusiast who
believed he could push me around. My recent conquest of
the dragon had instilled a newfound sense of courage
within me, and he said “Greetings, stranger. It might be
impolite to inquire, but where does your loyalty lies” he
said. “If you must know, I hail from a distant kingdom.
As for my loyalty, I am still seeking my path, but rest
assured, I do not harbor malevolence in my heart.” I said
gently trying to assert my stand of neutrality.
His voice, sweet and captivating, didn’t match his
imposing figure, and I found myself momentarily
entranced. However, I quickly shook off the enchantment,
for I knew I had to be cautious. Before I could continue
the conversation, he vanished from my vision, and a
strange force tugged at my shoulder blade. In an instant, I
found myself soaring upward, my trajectory an enigmatic
parabolic flight path. I glimpsed the vast expanse of the
land below, filled with both the promise of civilization
and the peril of uncertainty.
But there he was again, standing before me as if
defying the laws of nature, a testament to his
extraordinary abilities. Before I could react, his fist was
hurtling towards me with tremendous force. I instinctively
raised my hand to block, but the impact was
overwhelming. The air around him crackled with raw
energy as he launched me skyward once more. The pain
surged through me, and I could feel my health bar
diminishing rapidly.
Confusion and fear gripped my heart as I tried to make
sense of the situation. Was I truly nothing more than a
fragile creature, a mere puppet for this enigmatic gym
enthusiast to manipulate at will? The thought was
22
Threshold’s Pursuit
unsettling, but I refused to succumb to despair. With every
ounce of courage, I mustered a resolve to endure, to find
a way to counter this overwhelming force. I started
calculating my trajectory V02sin2Ø/2g or something I was
bad at physics even when my life depended on it ,as I was
trying to focus I saw that the small icons jumping on my
view increase but before I could focus on that I had an
idea. Determined to escape his relentless onslaught I tried
to change my trajectory by changing my drag by
spreading my hands , My parabolic trajectory now took
on a shuttle-like motion, and I saw a glimmer of hope—
an opportunity to reach a distant civilization that might
aid me in my plight. As I soared through the sky, aiming
for that elusive haven, he once again stood before me,
defying my calculations, his eyes gleaming with manic
intensity.
Desperation and frustration fueled my next plea.
“Stop!” I screamed, hoping to reason with him. But he
paid no heed, and his powerful fist collided with my face,
altering my trajectory and rendering my calculations
futile. With a bone-crushing impact, we crashed onto the
land below, he on top of me, the agony radiating through
my broken body.
As I lay there, amidst the whirlwind of physical pain
and the torment of my dire situation, a newfound clarity
washed over me. This battle transcended mere combat; it
was a clash of wills, a dance of egos. Vanquish this
formidable adversary, I realized I must employ his very
ego as a weapon to bring about his downfall.
“They slay one dragon and believe themselves
invincible,” he proclaimed with a malevolent grin,
jumping with force I didn’t knew muscles could gather
23
Teerth Sharma
soaring into the air and granting me a fleeting chance to
rise. But before I could respond, he started descending
upon me with the force of 9.8 meters per second squared,
a destructive trajectory I could ill afford to confront
directly. With a deep breath and focus, I recalled a time-
honoured tactic, revered across the realms of martial arts
in my world. Instead of moving away I have to face it head
on because this tactic was my only way to survive but
before I could pull the tactic off, I felt time slow down.
24
Threshold’s Pursuit
6. The Hound’s Ambush
As time seemed to slow its pace around me, a surreal
sight unfolded before my eyes. The man exuded not only
metaphorical mania but also an actual aura of anger,
casting a vivid crimson hue around his body. It reminded
me of the way my own movements were accompanied by
a gentle outline of blue. Yet, I found myself too
preoccupied with the onslaught of being thrown around to
fully grasp the power of my polarized vision, capable of
being wielded at will. Unfortunately, it offered no respite
in this moment as time merely slowed, refusing to halt
entirely.
Descending gracefully from the heavens, his ominous
approach conveyed an unsettling intention. It appears he
sought to crush my lungs or shatter my bones with a force
that bordered on absolute. The enigma lay in the details;
did he desire a painfully slow demise or was he not truly
intent on taking my life? Why perform such a grandiose
jump when he could have easily ended me by twisting my
neck as I lay defenseless on the ground? Amidst the
turmoil, there was no luxury of time to ponder his
motives, so I shifted my focus to the practicality of my
martial arts lesson, a Complimentary gift by Charlie.
As he drew nearer, a calm resolve washed over me,
and I adroitly shifted my balance to my back foot,
preparing for the encounter. In one swift motion, I seized
his chest and harnessed every ounce of strength within
me, skillfully combining the x vector with the y vector’s
acceleration to unleash a powerful twist. The effect was
25
Teerth Sharma
immediate; he was taken aback, unable to comprehend the
force of torque that brought him crashing to the ground.
Yet, the battle was far from over. My mind raced with
determination, and without hesitation, I sprang forward,
landing one foot on his chest. Gripping his outstretched
hand, I realized the strength I possessed—capable of
tearing his limb from his body. However, an internal
moral struggle prevailed. How could I, with any moral
authority, take another’s limb in such a way?
“Jerry, there’s no time!” I found myself shouting
aloud, reminding myself of the urgent reality at hand. This
man’s purpose was a mystery—he seems to be on a quest
to unravel the secrets of human aerodynamics or a
malevolent intent to end my life. And in this crucial
moment, I chose that I should act on the latter probability
Amidst the peculiar circumstances and the ethereal
slowing of time, I was still a coward I didn’t had the
strength to do anything right as I stood there his arm in
my hand the time regain its momentum and suddenly my
ace in the hole was gone “you should have teared it when
you had the chance”
In the midst of the tense situation, the mysterious
figure’s voice rang with an air of intrigue and enigma as
he tightened his grip on my forearm. His ethereal speed
was astonishing, leaving me breathless and bewildered by
his every move. Just as I thought I was about to fall into
his grasp, he deftly executed an unorthodox martial art
move, flipping me ahead of him and landing on my back
with his hand firmly gripping my neck.
“So, hero, are you going to reveal your true potential,
or are you just another failed experiment that the Hound
Unit is expected to dispose of?” His words flowed with an
26
Threshold’s Pursuit
unsettling gentleness, as if discussing the weather rather
than my imminent fate. I found myself uncertain of how
to respond, my hands instinctively raised in surrender
while his grip remained unyielding.
In a chilling laughter that sent shivers down my spine,
he addressed my confusion, “You see, I may be
considered a mere dog of the Supreme Commander, but
even Hounds like me have eyes and brains. I observed
your dramatic descent from the sky and witnessed your
raw strength as you took down that dragon. Combine that
with your lack of demi-human features, and it makes you
a prime suspect, fleeing to this Dissident Land.”
He sneered at the term “Dissident” and continued, “In
Hound Unit, we lack the abundance of lifeforce your
Royal Unit enjoys. Sacrificing our lifeforce grants us
strength, but unlike your impressive abilities, we don’t
receive any extraordinary gifts. Instead, I was bestowed
with the power to transform my limbs into monstrous
forms, perfect for dispatching any foe threatening our
Supreme Leader and motherland.”
His envy was evident, but I couldn’t fully grasp all the
complexities he spoke of. The notion of lifeforce being
relinquished and the idea of “gifts” eluded me.
Nonetheless, I decided to cooperate, hoping to find a way
out of this dire situation. “I am willing to comply, sir. Just
tell me what I need to do,” I said, attempting to sound as
persuasive as possible.
Mocking me, he responded, “Ah, now the royal kid
knows who’s in charge. Quite clever, unlike most
fugitives I encounter seeking asylum in these lands.” His
smile held a malevolent edge, hinting at his twisted
intentions. “You see, loyal as I am to our motherland, I’m
27
Teerth Sharma
quite tempted to sell you to the inhabitants of this land.
Breaking the refugee treaty will lead to war, and my
motherland will purify these lands in the name of the
Fourth.”
Curiosity and fear mingled as I dared to question him,
“But aren’t you human as well? And what’s with all this
talk about elves?”
For a moment, he seemed lost in his grandiose
schemes, ignoring my inquiries. Then, regaining focus, he
offered me a choice with a sinister tone, “You’ll wear an
iron collar, my friend, or you can choose the other
option.” His hand transformed into a menacing red claw,
making it clear that refusing his offer wasn’t an option to
be taken lightly.
As I contemplated my options, he vanished from my
sight and materialized behind me, his hand now resting on
my shoulder blade. The situation was dire, and my mind
raced for any glimmer of hope in this perilous encounter.
28
Threshold’s Pursuit

7. Bound By Chains.title: 7. Bound By Chains
7. Bound By Chains.text:
As the hound reappeared behind me, a profound sense
of despair sank into my very soul. His cold touch sent
shivers down my spine, and the iron collar he had placed
around my neck felt like a cruel reminder of my newfound
fate. Memories of the isekai I once read flooded my mind
– tales of suffering and oppression that had seemed distant
fantasies, now my horrifying reality.
The hound’s voice, deceptively sweet yet concealing a
rotten heart, commanded, “Hurry and start walking. We
will take about half a day to get there, and if we get any
later, I will personally make sure you don’t live to see the
upcoming war.” In this world, it appeared that slaves were
mere pawns in a vicious game of power and dominance,
their spirits crushed by a never-ending nightmare of
torment and defeat.
Despite my fear, I mustered the courage to respond,
my voice trembling with emotion. “Yes, sir, I am ready,”
I whimpered, trying to shield myself from his cruel kicks.
With each step, my heart grew heavier as I left behind the
life I once knew – a life of adventure and freedom, now
replaced with chains and servitude.
As we trudged through the dense forest, the weight of
reality bore down upon me. The beauty of nature
surrounding us only served to highlight the ugliness of the
world I had been thrust into. Each passing moment felt
like an eternity, and the possibility of escaping this
nightmare seemed ever more distant.
I gazed back longingly at the serene lakeshore, where
29
Teerth Sharma
I had once basked in the glory of a recent dragon slaying.
Oh, how the mighty had fallen, from dragon slayer to a
mere slave, bound to serve a malevolent master with
ambitions that threatened to engulf the world in flames.
Thoughts of my loved ones flooded my mind, and tears
welled in my eyes. How I yearned to see them once more,
to embrace them and express how much I loved them. But
here, in this cruel new world, all that awaited me was
suffering and loneliness. As we neared the city where my
fate would be sealed, hopelessness enveloped me. The
prospect of being auctioned off like an object made my
heart ache with sorrow. In this sorrowful world, my tears
were but drops in an ocean of despair. Before we could
reach the city gates, an enchanting facade that concealed
the ugliness within, I contemplated how this beautiful
exterior was meant to deceive and attract innocent people,
keeping them oblivious to the grim truth, serving as fuel
for a machine that fed on fear and innocence.
“We will turn east and walk until evening, alongside
the wall, until we reach the end of the eastern wall. There,
I have ties with the tower guards who are stationed every
200 meters,” the hound said, a tone of self-importance
underscoring his words, as if expecting gratitude for
sharing this information. My mind worked out the length
of the wall, estimating the distance as we walked. The sun
wasn’t in the afternoon stage, and it seemed like 11 am,
so if I had to walk until 6 pm, that would make it 7 hours
of walking. “Seven hours of walking at a speed of 5.1
km/hr makes it 35.7 km, just for one wall. This means the
city covers at least 1274.49 km2,” I said, my mind
calculating slowly.
“I don’t know what gibberish you are saying,” the
30
Threshold’s Pursuit
hound replied, glaring at me with his red eyes, seemingly
trying to discern the source of my knowledge, as if
surprised by my intelligence. “Are you privy to forbidden
knowledge, accessible only to scientists?” he asked, anger
seething in his voice, as he realized that my words might
have some logical basis. In his rage, his hand transformed
into a demonic form, a power bestowed upon him by some
unknown gift. He grabbed my hand and stabbed his nail
into my ulnar artery, causing blood to gush out, and I cried
out in pain. “What was that for?” I demanded.
“For uttering state secret knowledge in a non-secure
area. Next time you put my motherland in danger like this,
this bleeding will be from your neck,” he said, radiating
malevolence. I complied with his command and walked
until the sun fell. However, I soon realized my earlier
estimate of the wall’s length was incorrect. It seemed far
longer than I had calculated. As we continued walking, I
noticed that the wall was constructed entirely of white
limestone, intricately interconnected in a pattern
reminiscent of Roman engineering. Perhaps this land was
the Roman equivalent of a fantasy world.
“I know what you are thinking,” said the hound as the
sun set, and we approached our destination.
“And what might that be, sir?” I asked, employing a
hint of polite sarcasm.
“How the mighty empire came into a treaty with these
fugitives,” he replied, acknowledging my thoughts. It had
struck me as odd that there was no border or border police
from either side of this opposition, given the empire’s
apparent hatred for this land.
“I know that royal unit brats like you don’t leave the
labs until you have been given your gifts. However,
31
Teerth Sharma
hounds like us are picked from the border villages for
expertise in terrain, so I know a lot about this world far
more than you can imagine,” he said, pride glimmering in
his eyes as if he held superiority due to his knowledge. He
seemed unaware that he was the one selling me as a slave,
even as he provided information that might help me
escape this life of servitude.
“Sir, can you tell me why there is a treaty between our
motherland and this area?” I inquired, attempting to
maintain a polite tone.
“The first reincarnated hero – I’m sure you’ve heard
of him,” he asked, locking his gaze with mine. I knew that
denying knowledge would only provoke him further, so I
replied, “Yes, sir, he is my hero.” However, this response
seemed to trigger his rage, and he judo-flipped me and
seized my arm. “Take that back, or your arm will be taken
from you,” he said sternly.
“That was sarcasm, my dear sir,” I hurriedly
explained, recognizing that he despised the first
reincarnated hero.
“Oh, okay,” he said, demonstrating his lack of
intelligence outside the battlefield. He continued
speaking, recounting the hero’s actions, how he had killed
900 million monsters and used their souls to create a
system called the index of magic. This system granted
power to all races, even demi-humans. His tone oozed
disgust as he mentioned the demi-humans, revealing his
disdain for them.
“Additionally, the hero founded this Land of Half-
Breeds, which includes races like elves and vampires.
These races possess mana of a monster and manipulating
capabilities far superior to humans in our world. It makes
32
Threshold’s Pursuit
conquering this land incredibly difficult, as one of their
kind is equivalent to a thousand humans. However, our
motherland has produced us, the demons, capable of
matching and even surpassing these monstrous creatures.
We are the answer to their power,” he declared. But
before he could elaborate further, he halted, gazing at the
white wall before us. “Oh, I didn’t notice. We have
reached our destination,” he said with a glint of happiness
in his eyes he was pleased of himself. “so Now I get sold”
I mumbled.
33
Teerth Sharma
8. On The Wall’s Brim
As the hound stopped at our destination, he looked up,
his hair sweeping across his face. He brushed the strands
away and shouted, “Do not play games! Even though I do
not have mana sense, I can smell you, bastard!” A
different voice replied, “I hope you have the money,
hound. So, you better show me the respect I deserve, or I
can ring the alarm, and the whole Valkyrie unit will be
here.” The voice carried an alarming and aggressive tone,
and it was evident that no one liked the hound who had
bound me in chains.
“Oh, okay, I am sorry,” the hound said, now humbled
in his tone. It surprised me to see him show humility
towards the person he was speaking to, implying that this
person was even stronger than him. I turned my attention
to the figure approaching us, a black silhouette
descending from the wall. As the figure stood before me,
the evening light revealed a man as tall as me, but with
pale skin. His pupils appeared as a mesmerizing vertical
slit, while his sclera bore a haunting shade of grey, and his
irises emanated an intense, luminous yellow, exuding an
unmistakable aura of strength.
“So, who are you?” he asked, without looking directly
at me.
“I am the slave who is about to be sold,” I replied. His
interest seemed piqued as he was about to say something,
but then he paused. “Curious, but I want to see where this
takes you,” he said with a grin on his face. I turned to the
hound for an explanation.
34
Threshold’s Pursuit
“This is your majesty, Lord Sula de Koss, one of the
last 15 true vampires,” the hound explained. I gazed at the
newly introduced Lord, and he continued, “Please, call me
Koss.” There was an inexplicable power imbued in his
voice, almost commanding me to bend on one knee, and
instinctively, I complied, kneeling before him.
“Oh, I love it when an inferior blood bag like you
knows their place, but the same cannot be said for this
sour spot right over there,” said the vampire. As I looked
behind me, I saw the hound still standing up tight, looking
ready to fight at any moment. However, he knew that
clashing with the vampire would disrupt his own plot, so
he remained silent and knelt on one knee right next to me.
His face showed the utter disgust he was feeling while
bowing down.
“When faced with danger, animals have two instincts:
they either flock together or develop themselves to
overcome the threat,” the vampire said unironically. He
lifted himself off the ground gracefully, a sight that
seemed straight out of science fiction, but to be honest,
everything in this surreal place felt like it was from
another world.
Suddenly, he turned his back on us, facing the wall,
and zoomed over it, leaving us behind. “Follow on, blood
bag!” he shouted with a flicker of joy in his voice. The
hound looked at me as he stood up, offering his hand to
help me off the ground—a polite gesture I hadn’t expected
from him.
“You see, my friend, this is why we must eradicate
these so-called demi-humans,” he explained passionately.
“Our motherland has granted us, the hounds, the power to
stand on equal footing with those elusive elves. However,
35
Teerth Sharma
it is the might of our royal unit that can truly put vampires
back in their castles, even amidst the darkest shadows.
Believe in yourself, my friend, for you possess the power
to do good. I assure you, I never intended to harm you
during our duel. Instead, I sought to awaken your innate
gift. Sometimes, such gifts take time to manifest clearly,
but trust that the motherland’s wisdom guides us.”
He spoke with a hint of humility, as though recalling
an unsettling encounter with a bat that had left a lasting
impression on him. “I don’t have all night,” the lord’s
commanding voice called from high above, prompting the
hound to give a thumbs-up before bending his knees,
preparing for a tremendous leap.
As an athlete prepares to jump, he propelled himself
into the air, the first jump appearing within human
capabilities—around two meters high, a feat many could
achieve. However, what followed left me utterly
astonished. With raw power and unmatched strength, the
hound leaped once more, defying all expectations. The
ground itself seemed awestruck by his breathtaking
achievement as he soared, effortlessly scaling a colossal
20-meter-tall wall made of pristine white stone. His
muscular prowess evoked both fear and admiration.
Upon reaching the top, he called out, “Follow on,” as
if jumping such heights was a regular occurrence in this
extraordinary country. Yet, I knew better. It defied all
conventional logic to erect such a towering wall. My heart
raced with excitement and trepidation, for I had no idea
how to follow suit.
“How can I possibly jump this high?” I asked, my
voice tinged with uncertainty.
“Just jump,” he replied, a touch of irritation in his tone.
36
Threshold’s Pursuit
I sensed his impatience but also understood that he
believed in royal unit potential.
Taking a deep breath, I visualized myself surmounting
the obstacle before me. All doubts and fears seemed to
fade away as determination filled my veins. I summoned
every ounce of courage, channelling the power within me
that the hound had sought to awaken even though they
weren’t the one he expected this was my chance to be one
with the power I was given by the divine.
I attempted a jump, but it turned out to be nothing more
than pathetic. It seemed that all the muscular power within
me could barely propel me a mere 10 cm through the air.
I couldn’t help but question if some malfunctioning
powers were bestowed upon me, as I simply couldn’t
believe my incompetence with the abilities I had. Despite
never using them willingly, this feeling of inadequacy
haunted me.
In the midst of my self-doubt, the vampire approached
from the wall. He appeared to be indifferent to my
feelings as he spoke, “Even though the hound insists
you’re a mere pawn in this game, I doubt a pawn who
couldn’t even move one step at a time is of any use to the
motherland.” Tears welled up in my eyes he was targeting
me like he could read me he knew I wanted to feel needed,
as I gazed at him, realizing he was provoking me. He
wanted me to fight him, to take on the hound, though his
motivations remained unknown. While he might be an
ally, he was no friend; this vampire possessed a sinister
nature of his own. Although he wasn’t hunting me
presently, I knew the status quo could change for the
worse if I allowed my emotions to overpower me.
“I apologize, my lord, for finding myself in this
37
Teerth Sharma
situation, but I am merely a powerless slave. Please,
enlighten me on how such a pathetic being like myself can
overcome this wall,” I replied, laced with dripping
sarcasm. Such confrontations were not unfamiliar to me,
having dealt with haughty individuals during my school
days, a skill I had honed.
“Oh, I love it when people know their place,” he
replied with a proud expression. “Allow me to show you
the pinnacle of power there is.” His black and yellow eyes
gleamed in the night, exuding a predatory desire.
For a few seconds, I was bewildered, trying to grasp
the situation. When I felt myself lifted off the ground, I
knew something was amiss. It was then that I recognized
the person standing before me—a vampire. He
effortlessly wielded his telekinesis, and as I was taken up
by this eerie force of nature, he flew back up without a
care in the world. I found myself on the wall shortly after,
but those fleeting moments made me realize how
outclassed I truly was. The vampire possessed absolute
control over the x, y, and z vectors of this power, all
without even needing to look. Meanwhile, I couldn’t even
defeat a hound. Despite my fear, I understood that he
could snap my neck just as easily as he lifted me from the
ground.
“Weaker beings can be manipulated by those with
exponentially more mana than them. It’s simply a matter
of controlling the mana that one radiates from their skin.
For those who lack mana, this won’t work. So, don’t go
telling that hound that you flew up here by my telekinesis,
or he’ll surely catch on to the deception,” a voice echoed
in my head. I was certain it belonged to the vampire, yet
when I scanned my surroundings, he was nowhere to be
38
Threshold’s Pursuit
seen. Great, he could infiltrate my mind as well. Still, I
had no time to worry as I finally reached the top of the
wall.
39
Teerth Sharma

9. The Veil Of Melancholy.title: 9. The Veil Of Melancholy
9. The Veil Of Melancholy.text:
As I stood atop the wall, several things immediately
caught my attention. Firstly, the wall’s width was
designed to accommodate guards’ patrols, an obvious
feature. Secondly, as I gazed out at the city beyond, I was
struck by its grandeur—easily matching, if not
surpassing, the magnificence of the towering white wall.
A vast expanse of mansions with individual gardens
sprawled before me. Some structures exuded an ancient
charm, while others appeared as fresh as a newlywed
bride. Clearly, residing within these walls demanded a life
of grandeur, for everything about this place exuded
opulence. Each mansion had its own spacious garden,
unencumbered by concerns of space. They seemed to
prioritize living lavishly without any compromise.
I shifted my gaze downwards, and to my surprise, I
discovered a market lining the foot of the wall. The stalls
stretched all along the base, a lively and bustling setting.
However, a peculiar observation left me bewildered—the
entire market appeared to be in a state of standstill.
Despite the vibrant arrangement and placement of the
stalls, a profound silence enveloped the scene. Not a
single lamp illuminated any of the stalls, rendering the
area devoid of life.
The contrast between the lavish mansions and the
motionless market intrigued me. It seemed that while
grandeur and abundance thrived within the walls of the
mansions, an enigmatic stillness had befallen the
marketplace.
40
Threshold’s Pursuit
“If you’re done sightseeing, can we proceed, brat?”
called the hound from afar. As I looked in his direction, I
noticed he was inside a peculiar Bartizan, unlike any I had
seen before. It lacked the small holes usually present for
arrows and bullets. Nevertheless, I obeyed and started
walking towards it.
“I must understand; you are awestruck by the
architecture of our kingdom,” said the vampire as soon as
I entered the Bartizan, sporting a smile that seemed more
like a grin of supremacy. “Anyway, I am going to my
castle. The sun will dawn upon us in an hour. If you get
caught, I will simply say that I went home an hour early
because I am an endangered species,” he said with a
condescending tone. Strangely, he then pulled out a small
cylinder from his Sunday suit, shaped like a pocket watch.
“See,” he said, shoving the watch close to my nose. To
my surprise, I could read it clearly. The watch had an
obsidian face with three golden hands, and the numbers
were in Roman numerals made out of rubies. The time
displayed was 3:30, which meant two things: I had been
walking for much longer than I realized, and the definition
of “evening” needed a little adjustment.
“Okay, so I take my leave,” the vampire suddenly
declared, floating away from the gate I came from.
“This was a very strange behaviour of that bastard
vampire,” said the hound, watching the bat as it flew
away. “He was just rubbing in the fact that this kingdom
has something called ‘time,’ which they measure using
those strange circles. They once gifted our supreme ruler,
the son of the fourth, with this strange circle, and since
then, it has become a tradition for them to show their
circles every time they meet someone from our land,” he
41
Teerth Sharma
said, glancing at me. Then, he proceeded further inside the
Bartizan.
Inside, there was a bed, a room for a loo, and a
trapdoor. Knowing the hound well, I was certain he was
not going in there for the loo. “This city is guarded both
by magic and muscle power,” the hound said
mysteriously, opening the trapdoor. “The inner part of this
wall has a barrier so you can’t enter the city by leaping
over it. For mana-less beings like us, this is not a problem.
However, there is some sort of holy kingdom magic that
they acquired a few years ago, which guards the barrier as
well. My captain said that ‘if you want to enter the city,
you need to go through the trapdoor,’ which led to the idea
for the betterment of the motherland and the very idea that
has brought us here, my friend,” he explained.
But some of his words were beyond my understanding.
He proclaimed he was mana-less, yet the gods themselves
told me that mana was all-penetrating, and everyone had
it. Moreover, what is this holy kingdom? How many
political rivals are there, and if they all have such security
and manpower, why do they need someone like me to be
a slave?
The hound’s explanation left me with more questions
than answers, the ones he did not bother to answer. He
went into the trapdoor, and I, putting my anxiety away,
followed him. The trapdoor opened up to a long staircase
that led down to the ground level. The stairs themselves
were made of iron and painted to prevent rust, but how
the people around here had technology from our world
was a mystery to me. And I was no Sherlock.
As we went down the stairs, we were met by a wooden
door that looked worn out from years of usage. The hound
42
Threshold’s Pursuit
wasted no time in opening it. “We will go left from here
until the sun comes up, and then we’ll find the slave trader
there,” he said with certainty as we stepped out of the wall
and into the city. The air was filled with the scents of
various flowers and trees that thrived here. Taking in the
wondrous aroma, I turned to the hound and asked, “Shall
we start walking?” But before the hound could answer,
something shattered the peaceful ambiance the city
maintained. Suddenly, the market, which was still like
stone moments ago, was filled with lights, and numerous
black figures appeared from nowhere and everywhere. It
seemed like we had been caught in an ambush.
The situation took a dangerous turn, and I could feel
the tension in the air as we stood surrounded by these
mysterious figures. What would happen next, I could only
wonder.
The hound was no stranger to combat; his experience
and skills as a soldier were evident as soon as the black
figures appeared. With a swift transformation, his hands
turned into demonic forms. Without hesitation, he leaped
into the fray, meeting the attackers head-on.
His movements were like a deadly dance, as he
unleashed a barrage of punches, kicks, and grapples upon
his adversaries. Each strike was precise, aimed at
vulnerable spots that could incapacitate his opponents
swiftly. He twisted and turned, avoiding incoming attacks
with a combination of agility and instinct.
As the combatants closed in on him, he struck with
brutal efficiency. His fists connected with flesh and bone,
sending shockwaves of pain through the assailants. He
grabbed one by the neck, using their own momentum
against them and throwing them into another attacker.
43
Teerth Sharma
Another vicious kick sent another stumbling backward,
unable to regain their balance.
However, the fight took a gruesome turn as the
assailants began to regenerate. Despite the damage the
hound inflicted, they kept coming back, and their numbers
seemed to be increasing. Their resilience and eerie
persistence started to wear on the hound’s endurance.
Blood began to stain the ground, a testament to the
ferocity of the battle. The hound’s face was streaked with
sweat and determination, but also hints of exhaustion. He
fought on, unwilling to yield, but the relentless onslaught
was taking its toll.
With every strike he delivered, the assailants seemed
to come back stronger and more vicious. They pulled out
swords like katana which tore into the hound’s flesh,
leaving deep gashes that oozed crimson. Despite his best
efforts, he found it increasingly difficult to keep up the
fight.
The hound’s movements started to slow, and his once
precise strikes became more desperate. He was fighting
against the odds, but he refused to back down.
The assailants continued to swarm around him,
pressing their advantage. They sensed his weakening state
and capitalized on it, hoping to overpower the hound
completely. But even in the midst of adversity, the hound
never lost his determination, his will to survive, and
protect me.
In the face of overwhelming odds, the hound
continued to battle valiantly. His resilience and fighting
spirit were awe-inspiring, even in the midst of this
harrowing combat. But the question lingered – how much
longer could he hold out against this relentless onslaught?
44
Threshold’s Pursuit
The hound’s response came swiftly, cutting off any
further discussion. “I am a son of the motherland. I shall
not die without killing all of you!” he declared before
collapsing to the ground. His face flushed with anger,
blood oozing from his chest as the assailants closed in,
eager to finish him off. “This world demands sacrifices. I
will offer what’s left of my life and body for the greater
good of the motherland!” he bellowed, pulling his left arm
out and waving it in the air. An intense aura enveloped
him, reminiscent of the one I had witnessed in the
presence of the angel. The surrounding area grew hotter,
the middle of the night seemingly ablaze. Before I could
react, a feminine voice echoed from behind me.
“Oh no, you don’t!” she yelled. As I looked at her, she
was gorgeous with her stunning reddish-brown hair. Her
regal black gown was a masterpiece of elegance and
sophistication. The rich, velvety fabric clung to her figure
like a second skin, accentuating her curves with allure. Its
obsidian hue absorbed the surrounding light, creating an
aura of mystery and intrigue. As she walked, a radiant
aura of power seemed to emanate from her. But I feared
it was too late, for the area was turning unbearably warm
. Yet, the lady suddenly halted in her tracks, ready to
intervene and confront the impending danger with her
whatever she got
She gracefully waved her hands, and in an instant, she
was surrounded by a mesmerizing display of circles,
spheres, and triangles. Each ethereal shape exuded a
radiant blue hue, as if imbued with otherworldly power.
With a pointed finger, the woman directed her magic
towards the hound’s golden aura. The shapes transformed
into mysterious letters, some familiar like alpha and
45
Teerth Sharma
omega, while others remained enigmatic. These letters
surged towards the hound, seemingly inscribing an
ancient script in the air. I braced myself for a fiery
explosion, but instead, the flames merged with the
mystical words, creating a protective wall of energy
around him. It was an extraordinary spectacle, an awe-
inspiring display of magic beyond comprehension.
After completing her task of stopping the hound’s
impending explosion, she turned her back, ready to leave.
But then, she suddenly looked back, her gaze intense.
“There is another source of mana, feeble compared to
mine, but a lot more than what you guys can handle here,”
said the lady to the black assailants. Turning back to face
them, she stepped into the light, and I could see her
features more clearly. Her sharp, Asian-like features were
mesmerizing. Her eyes glowed in the darkness, a
captivating mix of red and white unlike the lord vampire’s
black and yellow irises.
As I stood there, she approached me, gently touching
my cheek with her finger, “So, you are the mana source I
was looking for.” Her voice was alluring, and I was
spellbound. “But Koss’ reports say you are just a lowly
soldier boy who underwent the same process as the
hounds. Yet, you possess mana beyond the level of half-
elves.” She smiled at me before turning her attention back
to the assailants, whom she referred to as half-elves.
“Take him to the interrogation room!” she commanded,
and suddenly, darkness enveloped me as I lost
consciousness.
46
Threshold’s Pursuit

10. Dance Of Politics.title: 10. Dance Of Politics
10. Dance Of Politics.text:
As I slowly regained consciousness, I found myself
facing a grey wall in a dimly lit cell. The words etched on
the wall revealed the absence of the universal declaration
of human rights. “I am innocent, it’s been 5 days, they
torture,” read one of many petroglyphs left by previous
prisoners. Voices echoed from multiple directions.
“Please, I am innocent!” cried a woman. “You are
innocent, then I am a human!” shouted a man, followed
by the sound of whipping, intensifying the despair.
Unable to bear their cries for help, I shifted my focus and
rose to my feet, taking in the bleak surroundings. The
classic iron grid blocked any view beyond, encapsulating
the hopelessness within. In this grim place, uncertainty
loomed—how long would I be trapped in this abyss of
sorrow and suffering? The echoes of torment persisted,
haunting reminders of the sombre reality.
Before I could indulge in a poetic battle of thoughts
about how this prison treated immigrants, especially me,
poorly and how I might escape, I heard the cliché iron
grid’s lock opening. My attention turned to that direction.
“So, this is the mystery that the witch was talking about,”
proclaimed a feminine voice before entering my cell. As
she stepped inside, in the dark I could barely make out her
features, even those left me in awe. Her presence exuded
an aura of authority and power, contrasting with the
gloom of the prison.
As she slowly approached, I got a complete view. She
wore a formal white shirt adorned with medals, signifying
47
Teerth Sharma
her high rank. Her black pants and matching boots exuded
authority. Her sharp features resembled a stereotypical
Californian beauty. However, it was her ears that caught
my attention, and suddenly, everything made sense.
She was an elf; her pointy ears confirmed that I was in
a kingdom where their race held superiority over me. Her
two-toned hair added to her enigmatic presence, with
black roots flowing down to her chin, and the rest of her
hair shimmering in a captivating shade of green,
reminiscent of the lush trees they grew up in this walled
up city.
Mesmerized by her beauty, I found myself entrapped
as she firmly gripped my t-shirt’s collar. “So, convict,
before we cut your head off and be done with this mess,
speak what you must,” she demanded. Attempting to meet
her gaze was a challenge, given her towering height of at
least half a foot over me. Fear of a brutal demise lingered,
casting a shadow over my every thought.
“I am innocent!” I yelled, my voice echoing through
the cold, dimly lit cell. Despite knowing my plea might be
dismissed as clichéd, I couldn’t suppress the desperation
in my tone. The prison director, a tall and imposing elf
with stunning two-toned hair, grabbed my t-shirt’s collar,
her eyes gleaming with authority.
“We are well aware of your innocence,” she stated, her
voice carrying an air of otherworldly wisdom. “But what
we seek to unravel is the purpose of keeping you alive
amidst the impending war. You, the enigmatic catalyst of
this brewing conflict, has manage to put fate of our great
nation, Seyleprus on risk. As the prison director, the
burden of our realm’s future rests upon my shoulders, and
I refuse to indulge in needless bloodshed. Our heritage,
48
Threshold’s Pursuit
built on the sacred principles of co-existence and peace,
guides our every action.”
Her words hung in the air like a haunting melody,
weaving an intricate tapestry of fate.
In the midst of her anger, I dared to pose a question,
my voice carrying an air of curiosity tinged with
apprehension. “You claim to know of my innocence, yet
you accuse me of being the one who brewed this war,” I
inquired, attempting to sound composed despite the
mounting tension in the air.
Her steely gaze locked onto mine as she spoke with
regal authority, “Indeed, your innocence is apparent from
the intactness of your mana, ruling out any association
with the heinous Regenspire dictatorship scientists and
their gruesome experiments. You are not from
Regenspire; that much is clear. The only plausible
explanation is that you and the hound are the product of a
new political alliance between the holy empire and the
dictatorship. Or, intriguingly, you may hail from another
world entirely. Your attire, the garments worn in the holy
empire are too different these clothes you worn is a local
specialty of our nation.”
Her words ignited a whirlwind of thoughts in my mind,
as I grappled with the implications of her deductions. My
cover was blown, and now I stood at a crossroads. I could
either reveal the truth about being a god’s agent and risk
prosecution as a war criminal, or I could maintain my
facade and hope they dismiss my claim as delusional
ramblings.
Summoning my courage, I decided honesty was the
best course. “I am what you may call a god’s agent, sent
here by divine will to repair and recover the holy bridge
49
Teerth Sharma
that connects your realm to the divine,” I confessed,
hoping my words would find some understanding.
To my surprise, the elven woman seemed to entertain
the idea. “Yes, that can be a sensible deduction. So, we
must take you to the matriarch of the elven council,” she
declared, turning to her hidden guards.
Before the guards could act, another voice, familiar
and haunting, echoed in the dimly lit chamber. “Why not
to the matriarch of the vampiric council?” It was Lord
Koss, the vampire who had captured me.
A tense exchange ensued as the two formidable beings
engaged in a verbal duel, their words laced with pride and
power. Koss seemed eager to represent me before his
matriarch, while the elven woman remained resolute in
her duty to her matriarch.
Amidst the political turmoil, I interjected, “Why not
take me to both of them? Surely, as the supreme members
of your respective councils, they must meet to discuss this
matter.”
The suggestion hung in the air as they both regarded
me. “Newcomer, you don’t know much about politics, do
you?” the elf director inquired, her tone condescending.
“You see, blood-bag,” Lord Koss chimed in, using his
usual derogatory term, “this kingdom was built with a
specific system. All demi-humans form a council called
the Council of the Kingdom. However, elves and
vampires are granted special privileges. They form their
own council with the right to vote on matters brought
before the Council of the Kingdom. Additionally, they
can interject if they believe the Council of the Kingdom
is mistaken. Moreover, during times of peace, the elves
hold supreme authority, as they are known as the most
50
Threshold’s Pursuit
peace-loving race in this world.”
“But when war is on the horizon,” he continued, his
tone taking on a more serious note, “the vampires take the
lead and assume complete control over both councils. The
matriarch of the vampires holds the highest position,
hailing from the noblest of vampire royal dynasties.”
“The matriarch of elves also hails from the noblest of
elven dynasties, the Lady of Flowers, Lady Elfine Do ‘elf
Elfona,” she replied, her voice filled with reverence, as if
the mere mention of the name could bring prosperity to
the entire realm. “In times of war, you should indeed turn
to the vampires, as they possess unmatched strength and
power. However, during the day, when the sun shines, we
still need the elves to fight alongside us. Their affinity for
peace is essential to balance the power dynamics. But as
night falls, the vampires shall reign superior, for we are
the strongest species of them all.”
His words painted a vivid picture of the intricate dance
between the elves and vampires, their roles intertwined in
the delicate balance of power as lord Koss said with proud
in his eyes.
“Are you forgetting the grand liches?” reminded the
elf, her voice tinged with an air of superiority.
“They went extinct due to the joint effort of our
kingdom and the dictatorship,” said the vampire,
acknowledging the truth.
The elf smiled, feeling triumphant. “The very
dictatorship that wants us extinct. They are behind this
demon issue. They must be using the bodies of grand
liches and arch liches, using them as catalysts to drain
mana from humans.”
The vampire tried to maintain his composure. “I won’t
51
Teerth Sharma
deny that possibility, but it took 1000 arch liches together
to bring down our last king. And his daughter, the
strongest of the royal vampires, remains a force to be
reckoned with.”
The elf, finally sounding defeated said “Don’t forget
about her husband.”
Lord Koss’s voice resonated with a touch of reverence
as he uttered the words that echoed through the ages,
“Well, of course, no one can forget her husband, the
mightiest being in the entire universe, and the sole
benefactor of this kingdom. But do not, I repeat, do not
forget that he transcended into something greater a being
beyond mortal limits. The legendary First Hero, a figure
of unparalleled grandeur, turned himself into a royal
vampire.”
As the words hung in the air, the atmosphere seemed
to shift, as if paying homage to the immortal legend. The
mention of the First Hero, a revered and celestial figure,
sent shivers down the elf’s spine, and she appeared to be
finally defeated. For in the heart of every inhabitant of this
kingdom, the First Hero was not just a hero but a divine
embodiment of power and wisdom—a figure whose very
existence shaped the destiny of this realm or so I
understood from the bits of information I got from both
elf and the hound.
“If I heard correctly, vampires are immortal beings, so
the First Hero must still be among us,” I asked eagerly,
the prospect of meeting such a legendary figure filling me
with excitement.
“You heard right but,” replied the vampire with a hint
of sorrow in his tone. “The First Hero was a summoned
agent of the gods, much like you. But he was not merely
52
Threshold’s Pursuit
reincarnated here once; he was brought to this world
twice, destined to shape its very destiny. He is also the
progenitor of the Index of Magic. Upon completing his
sacred mission, he was sent back to wherever he came
from, and we eagerly await his third return.”
The melancholic tone in the vampire’s voice hinted at
a profound longing, as if the only thing that pained him
was the distance that kept him from the presence of the
revered First Hero.
In a sudden shift of atmosphere, Lord Koss turned his
attention to the wall, lost in thought for an unspecified
period. As he turned back to face me, his tone carried an
air of determination. “We shall take you to the Joint
Council,” the vampire declared, his words resonating with
authority.
I couldn’t help but wonder how this though had
reached me while fighting over politics. The director, now
visibly concerned about the breach, questioned, “Isn’t this
cell warded against transmission magic and all other
forms of sorcery? Pray tell how did you got a message
down here lord Koss.”
