Smirking with confidence, Lord Koss responded, “The
transmission came from none other than the Sorceress of
Annihilation. Mere magic warding crystals hold no power
against her. She can bend the very fabric of reality with
her enchantments.”
The director, conceding to the Sorceress’s prowess,
left the cell, perhaps to attend to matters beyond.
“Now, my bloodbag,” Lord Koss addressed me with a
hint of amusement, “it seems it’s time for you to meet the
esteemed Joint Council.”
Suddenly I was engulfed with darkness.
53
Teerth Sharma

11. The Enigmatic Gathering.title: 11. The Enigmatic Gathering
11. The Enigmatic Gathering.text:
As the darkness slowly subsided, I found myself
standing in yet another dimly lit chamber, but this room
held a peculiar and enchanting allure. Bathed in an
ethereal blue tint, everything from the grand pillars to the
castle-like walls seemed to exude an otherworldly
essence. Yet, as I glanced up, I realized that the true
source of this enchanting hue was the resplendent
chandelier suspended from the high ceiling.
The chandelier was unlike any I had ever seen before,
reminiscent of something straight out of a fantastical tale.
Its intricate design featured elaborate patterns and delicate
carvings it had blue flame burning on it which were the
reason of the ethereal blue tint.
As I began to notice the features around, I wondered
why I was alone in this room. Just moments ago, I had
been a prisoner. Did they suddenly gain trust in me, or
was this place truly inescapable? The possibility of escape
lingered in my mind, but before I could explore it further,
the room began to shake violently, as if struck by an
earthquake. The unsettling sensation reminded me of the
reality plane I had encountered before meeting the god.
Amidst the tremors, I heard a peculiar noise,
mechanical and rhythmic, like the turning of gears. My
curiosity piqued, I followed the sound and found myself
facing a round table. The table exuded an air of
importance and was adorned with intricate details,
reminiscent of the legendary Arthurian round table.
I was sure there was no table at the centre before I
54
Threshold’s Pursuit
heard the noise but before I could investigate further, a
sudden wave of fear washed over me, causing my knees
to buckle beneath me. I crashed to the ground, my nose
stinging from the impact. It was a familiar feeling, one I
had experienced before, triggered by the memory of a past
encounter that still haunted me.
Despite my attempts to fight it, my heart raced, and my
body refused to obey my rational thoughts. It was as if my
primal instincts were taking control, urging me to
surrender to the overpowering fear.
A familiar voice which sounded annoyed questioned
my worthiness, “If you are this weak, why did the
Almighty even choose you?” As I looked up, I saw the
gorgeous woman I had met just before my hound
companion’s demise. Attempting to impress her, I replied,
“God chose me because He saw my willpower,” although
deep inside, I knew she was leagues above me.
Suddenly, my vision changed once more, similar to the
event at the lake’s shore. It had not occurred since then,
leaving me astonished. I gazed at my hand, still radiating
a blue aura, but when I looked at the lady before me, her
aura appeared even deeper crimson than the hound’s,
whom I had considered a psychopath. Realizing that hers
must be at least double that, I could not help but feel a mix
fear and awe.
“Oh, you do have the eye of judgment, don’t you,” said
another voice, but it held an uncanny quality. It seemed to
resonate in a frequency beyond the natural, yet I could
hear her with eerie clarity. The nature of power exuded
from her words, sending shivers down my spine. I dreaded
meeting the speaker, but fate had different plans as she
emerged from the darkness. Her skin was as pale as snow,
55
Teerth Sharma
and her eyes bore a striking combination of colours—
black for the sclera and a dark, ruby-red for the iris. The
crimson hue was deeper than any shade I had ever seen,
yet it shined with an unsettling brilliance. As my gaze
fixated on her, I could not sense her aura, but her features
were starkly apparent. The moment her eyes laid on my
mortal body, I noticed a black aura radiating from her,
sending chills down my spine. Despite her terrifying aura
and striking eyes, her clothes were surprisingly ordinary.
She looked like a teenager, dressed in a simple
ensemble—a black pair of jeans and a black t-shirt. Her
appearance contradicted the immense power she exuded.
With a rather robust body, she seemed unassuming in her
attire, but there was an undeniable air of authority about
her that left me unsettled. The contrast between her
youthful appearance and the overwhelming presence she
emanated made her all the more enigmatic and
intimidating.
“Do not bully the chosen agent of God, for we might
face punishment on the day of our final judgment,” said a
voice that resonated with beauty and perfection. As the
third figure emerged into the blue light, I immediately
recognized her—it was the prison director. Confusion
filled my mind as I wondered why she was here. “Please
tell me this is still a prison, and I am sure you don’t allow
visitors to kill prisoners, do you?” I pleaded, still lying on
the ground, my knees refusing to obey any command.
“Oh, darling, I think you’ve confused me for my
younger sister. But do not worry, no one will dare touch
you in my presence,” said the person who claimed to be
the elder sister of the prison director. I felt a mixture of
relief and trepidation in her presence, my eyes saw that
56
Threshold’s Pursuit
her aura had a tint of green I felt relieved as I knew
nothing bad can come from the colour of nature. “Please
save me,” I implored, hoping that she could indeed offer
some assistance dumb as it sounded, I felt she was the
only person I could trust in this room right now.
Immediately, the air pressure surged, and her body was
surrounded by a vibrant green light, illuminating the
entire room with an otherworldly glow. It was evident that
she possessed an incredible strength. “I ask the Witch of
Annihilation to put down the barrier she placed, which is
making Sir Hero unable to stand on his knees. For
whatever authority you vampires hold during war, the war
is yet to begin,” she declared with a firmness that
commanded respect.
“All right, Queen, I’m sorry,” said the brown-haired
person, her tone heavy with sarcasm, as she clicked her
fingers. Instantly, my knees found strength, and with the
elf’s assistance, I rose slowly. “Sir hero, you have the
backing of 1 million elves, so no need to worry,” she
whispered gently in my ear.
“So, Your Majesty, the great Elfona, do your divine
eyes reveal his origin, or are they a mere ruse to deceive
vampires?” questioned the lady in jeans, dripping with
sarcasm. “I am well aware they work, sensing his mana
surpasses that of a human. My queen, doubting his hero
status would be foolish. Let’s ask him why he’s here and
if he is a foe to the kingdom or an ally.” her voice made it
clear that she wanted me to be a foe for the kingdom said
the witch of Annihilation with humbleness
“Sir Hero, we are considered the three strongest in this
kingdom. I am Elfona, the Queen of Elves,” she
introduced herself, gesturing towards the woman beside
57
Teerth Sharma
her. “This is Liza Nightshade, the Director of both the
School of Mages and the Mage Guild, bearing the
legendary title of Witch of Annihilation.” As soon as Liza
was acknowledged, I witnessed her encircled by ethereal
magical constructs of various shapes, captivatingly
rotating around her. She surely knew how to make a
dramatic entrance.
“And this,” Elfona continued, pointing to the girl in the
black shirt and jeans, “is Sybilla von Nyx Hecate
Darkcrest the matriarch of Vampires and also the wife of
the first hero bearing the legendary title of Night’s
Sovereign ,” the elves chimed in, affirming her grand
status. However, Sybilla immediately interjected, “Please
call me Hecate, unless we are in front of the whole
council, then you may address me as Majesty or Count
Sybilla.” Her voice was no longer the strange, magical
tone she used before but rather a cute and pleasant one. I
quickly reminded myself not to entertain any
inappropriate thoughts, considering vampires’ mind-
reading abilities.
58
Threshold’s Pursuit

12. Catalyst Of The Kingdom.title: 12. Catalyst Of The Kingdom
12. Catalyst Of The Kingdom.text:
As I pondered my opinion of these three remarkable
women, they each took their predetermined seats, leaving
one spot unoccupied. “Now, let us discuss the matter at
hand between the two of you before we decide the fate of
Sir Hero,” asserted Elfona with an air of authority.
“It appears the pressing matter is the Regenspire’s
hound meeting its demise in our kingdom,” offered
Hecate, her voice filled with conviction.
Liza, sounding like the strategist, chimed in, “Indeed,
we could claim the hound invaded, but alas, we lack
concrete evidence. We dare not rely on Lord Koss, for his
revelation of betraying the hound’s party as a double
agent would cast doubt upon his loyalty and furthermore,
he is the only source of information we got inside hound
right now and you know how much illegal trade we let
happen to get that.”
The burden of dictatorship did not concern her. She
looked over-confident, Elfona continued, “The
suppressive tactics they might employ will not deter us.
They dare not let this mess surface, as it would unveil
their inhumane experiments, thereby forfeiting support
from the holy kingdom.”
Together, they weighed the ramifications of their
actions.
As I observed the situation, relief washed over me
knowing that it would not escalate into an all-out war,
sparing countless lives from needless tragedy. Yet, the
gravity of the matter could not be overlooked, and I knew
59
Teerth Sharma
it would not be taken lightly.
“I believe the origin of this plan lies not with the
hound, but rather with his captain, who instigated the
entire scheme,” I ventured, hoping to contribute
meaningfully and make a favourable impression.
Suddenly, Liza rose from her seat, offering her support.
“Did the hound explicitly state this?” she inquired, her
eyes glimmering with hope. “Yes, he made it quite clear,”
I replied with pride, hoping to impress the formidable
Witch of Annihilation.
Elfona interjected, “Then we have yet another pressing
matter at hand. The captain of the hound unit is likely
closing in to avenge the loss of their fallen comrade.” Her
words hung heavily in the air. “Though such an act won’t
garner official support from the dictatorship or so they
would say if this ever to come out, but it is highly probable
that the entire hound unit is headed our way.”
“We find ourselves at a disadvantage, for while we are
formidable, this battle might elude our grasp with only 50
half-elf soldiers at our disposal,” Hecate lamented, her
tone tinged with sadness. My quest for hope led me to
interject, “But I thought you three were the strongest
among all!”
Liza gently revealed the truth, “True, individually, we
possess great power, but our hands are bound by multiple
restrictions. Elfona, as the holy queen, cannot engage in
combat, lest it triggers an all-out war from the elf’s side.
For myself, a confrontation risks war with the holy
empire, given my position as the director of the mage
guild and mage school - striving to maintain neutrality. As
for Hecate, unlike us her hand is laden with more
problems if she were to fight the war will begin from both
60
Threshold’s Pursuit
ends holy kingdom and the dictatorship together.”
“Why can’t you, Hecate, find a way to fight despite the
politics?” I inquired, seeking a solution to her
predicament. Her response was unequivocal, “My mana
is boundless and holy empire have built something called
sensors which detect this and alarm them, and attempting
to restrain it would be futile once I activate my title for an
all-out battle and without mana I am as strong as you are
and you know how that fares our Infront of hounds,
immediately breaking the three kingdom treaty article 4.”
As I wrestled with my limited knowledge of their
world, I grappled with the complexity of the situation,
trying to conceive a plan that would not ignite war on any
front. However, the intricacies of their challenges seemed
insurmountable.
“I have a solution,” declared Elfona, her voice
brimming with both determination and delight. Hecate,
though not visibly pleased, could not hide the sparkle in
her eyes as she inquired, “What might that be?”
“Liza, being the most skilled mage among us, can
utilize her expertise to activate the advanced stage index
of magic of our Sir hero. With this newfound power, Sir
Hero can fight for our cause as the righteous hero he is,”
Elfona proposed, her gaze shifting towards me with a hint
of remorse, acknowledging that I might feel coerced into
siding with them. However, my loyalty was unwavering
even before I suicided thus, I declared boldly, “I will
always support you all.”
The plan seemed flawless, as Hecate envisioned, “This
approach could work exceedingly well. If Sir Hero were
to betray us, it would offer me the opportunity to unleash
my limitless mana and potentially obliterate both him and
61
Teerth Sharma
the dictatorship in a single decisive move.”
The room buzzed with a sense of hope and unity as the
women embraced their roles in the intricate web of fate
that lay ahead. I, too, felt a surge of purpose, ready to
stand alongside them in this precarious endeavour, guided
by their wisdom and courage.
“Then I must take him to the temple, but does he have
the catalyst, or will the nation provide him one?” asked
Liza.
“What does a catalyst mean in this context? Do I need
H2SO4?” I asked with a touch of sarcasm.
“Catalyst, in this case, refers to anything you have
gained from your exploits or the exploits of your
predecessors as you are one of the gods agents. It will be
sacrificed to the system, combined with your life force
and an instigator’s mana, granting you the advanced index
of magic,” Liza explained, highlighting her adept
teaching skills.
“Before we head to the temple, could you please
explain to me what the system entails and why sacrifice
holds such significance in this world?” I asked Liza, using
my most pleading puppy eyes, knowing she could not
resist them.
Liza began to unravel the profound workings of their
world, “When the divine beings created this world, they
established a balanced system with one fundamental rule:
to maintain equilibrium at all times. So, if one desires
something significant, like riches, they must offer
something of equal value in return. The balance then
grants their request, and this fundamental principle
governs how this world functions. Taking advantage of
this system, Lord First sacrificed the lifeforce of nine
62
Threshold’s Pursuit
hundred million lives to request the creation of the ‘index
of magic.’ This system has two stages devised by him.”
“The first stage grants the person the ability to reveal
their own mana reserve and health to others. You might
even be able to perceive it slightly if you concentrate and
squint your eyes,” Liza explained.
“The second stage, however, is more remarkable. It
endows the individual with the ability to perceive the
mana and health of anyone with mana. Moreover, they
can attain titles and levels if they are proficient in the
process. Titles are granted by the index of magic when
someone achieves something nearly impossible. Once
acquired, these titles can be kept hidden or revealed, each
providing unique buffs. For example, when revealed my
title, ‘Witch of Annihilation,’ grants me the blessings of
boundless mana and 500 times enhanced destructive
power this is also the application of the equilibrium I give
them important information like what my capability are
which can be disadvantageous for me but this title makes
me stronger the more information I give so is the
fundamental work of title. As for levels, Lord First
relinquished his boundless capabilities to embrace
limitations in the form of a level cap. Each level gained
makes him a hundred times stronger, we further copied
his example and gave away all our boundlessness to be
stronger with a level cap and all three of us here are at the
level cap and most of the citizen of our nation have
activated the advance index of magic “ she clarified.
Liza’s exceptional teaching skills left me in awe,
realizing why she was the revered director of a prestigious
school. Her ability to convey complex information with
clarity and her insights into what I wanted to know were
63
Teerth Sharma
truly awe-inspiring.
“Can we get started on this process right away?” I
eagerly inquired, determined to become as formidable as
them and curious about the title I might gain. Elfona
responded, sharing my enthusiasm, “As soon as we find a
suitable catalyst for you.”
Suddenly, inspiration struck, and I remembered an
extraordinary feat from my journey to this realm. “Wait,
I have something in mind,” I exclaimed. Reaching into
my pocket, I retrieved a dragon’s claw, a trophy from
when I had slain a dragon during my descent into this
world. Holding it up, I asked, “Will this be sufficient as a
catalyst?”
The air in the room seemed to hold its breath as the
others examined the dragon’s claw. Elfona and Hecate
exchanged glances, contemplating its potential as the
catalyst I needed “Liza I think this will work” said Hecate
showing it to Liza and I saw a visible concern in
everyone’s face there they looked almost scared.
64
Threshold’s Pursuit

13. The Unseen Path.title: 13. The Unseen Path
13. The Unseen Path.text:
As I stood there, awaiting the trio’s next move, Liza
approached me and gently clasped my hand. “Now, hold
on tightly, my dear,” she said, her smile radiating warmth.
However, in an instant, her smile vanished, and so did she,
along with the entire world around me. Darkness
enveloped me, leaving me in a state of utter disarray for
what felt like an eternity. The minutes passed slowly, like
an eternal void, until suddenly, the world materialized
once again.
The transition was abrupt as I found myself inside the
what looked like Cathedral, a sense of awe overwhelmed
me. The vast expanse of the sacred hall was adorned with
intricate carvings, sparkling stained glass windows, and
grand pillars reaching for the heavens. The air felt charged
with ancient magic, and the soft glow of mystical runes
cast ethereal patterns on the marble floor everything had
emphasis on the word grand.
As I looked around, I heard a gentle sound, and
suddenly, Liza was there before me. Intrigued by her
teleportation ability, I couldn’t help but ask, “How do you
manage to teleport like this? It’s an incredibly powerful
skill to have.”
Liza smiled gently as she explained, “There are two
methods of teleportation. The first method involves using
teleportation marks within a room. By synchronizing
those marks with the corresponding frequency in another
location, the data is transferred, allowing the body to be
disassembled and reassembled at the destination.”
65
Teerth Sharma
“The second method, which I used to free you from
prison, is more personal,” she continued. “It involves
synchronizing your frequencies with mine, pulling you
out of that specific space and time, and then aligning you
with my own frequency.”
Her explanation fascinated me, revealing the
intricacies of mage craft. Then she asked, “does mage
craft interest you”, I confessed, “I didn’t have magic in
my world.”
“Magic is just like any other power. While magic may
be common here, good-willed humans aren’t, and not all
demi-humans possess benevolence either. Mana is akin to
energy; you had something called nuclear energy in your
world, right? It could have been used for betterment, but
you know how that worked,” Liza said with a touch of
melancholy in her voice, as if she could personally relate
to the consequences.
Curiosity prompted me to inquire, “But how do you
know about my world?”
She blushed slightly before revealing, “Lord First was
my teacher, and he often compared me to a hydrogen
bomb when scolding me. He had extensive vocabulary for
scolding.”
This revelation deepened my curiosity about Lord
First’s identity. “How did you know that I belong to that
particular Earth where your lord first came from?” I
asked, remembering that God himself said that the
existence of a multiverse is the actual truth.
“Do you know how these multiple universes exist
together? You might think there is a place outside the
multiverse and that it would be stretched out like galaxies,
but that’s not right,” Liza explained.
66
Threshold’s Pursuit
Curious, I asked, “Then what is right, Liza?”
“The multiple universes or the multiverse are vibrating
at different frequencies, which keeps them apart. What
God did was create spaces within these frequency gaps
and use them as bridges and more for his own
entertainment. Do you know how we are being kept in this
universe right now?” she inquired.
“I guess by matching my frequency to something in
here,” I replied, trying to figure out the connection.
Liza clarified, “Your frequency was matched with the
‘bridge,’ which is actually supposed to be the only non-
changing thing in creation. It is designed so that divines
can match their frequency with our universe.”
Then, she blinked at me, and suddenly, it dawned on
me. “That’s exactly like teleportation! So, were you able
to see where my frequency was synced with, guessing my
origin?” I asked, realizing she might have discovered my
identity through this process.
“Of course not. It was your accent that made it obvious
you hailed from the same place as Lord First, you idiot.
But what I was getting at was the fact that your original
frequency was etched into the bridge. This will allow you
to return to your world, and Elfona saw these etching,
confirming that you are from Lord’s world,” she said,
laughing at my cluelessness.
Embarrassed yet amused, I blushed. Talking to a
female after so long was an unexpected and intriguing
experience.
“Wait, did you just say accent?” I asked Liza, seeking
confirmation.
“Yes, your accent and way of speaking, along with
certain words like ‘frequency,’ all of them match Lord
67
Teerth Sharma
First’s accent,” she replied, unaware of the significance of
her observation.
“If our accents match, he must be from my country!” I
exclaimed loudly, a surge of excitement rushing through
me. Her eyes widened, and the red iris revealed the full
extent of her beauty.
In a rush, Liza said, “When you say, ‘same country,’
is it possible you had someone like... wait, follow me!”
With urgency, she darted towards a dark corridor inside
the grand cathedral. I followed, matching her pace, as we
moved towards the heart of the sacred place. Suddenly,
she came to a halt, but in my haste, I accidentally bumped
into her soft, pillow-like body. Her hair emitted a pleasant
scent, further distracting me.
“Well, look up,” she said, realizing it was too dark to
see anything. In a swift motion, she clapped her hands,
and the room lit up. Before me, I saw a face that left me
awestruck.
A white marble construct of a man stood before me,
with sharp features, a pronounced jawline, and a
handsome face. Though I couldn’t be certain if he was
from my original country, I believed that with a colour
photo or some additional evidence, I might be able to
ascertain the truth.
“I know it’s larger than life, but right now, this is the
only thing we have,” Liza said, sensing my
disappointment.
“Don’t worry about that. I’m sure they didn’t have
cameras back then,” I reassured Liza, understanding the
depth of her emotions and the nostalgia she felt for her
master.
In that moment, I realized the connection between Liza
68
Threshold’s Pursuit
and Lord First, the teacher she cherished deeply. The bond
they shared transcended time and space, and I couldn’t
help but feel a profound sense of respect and admiration
for their relationship, and sadness for their undue
separation.
“We have no time to waste,” said Liza, her demeanour
now focused and determined. “Follow me.” She grabbed
my arm, and I obediently followed her. To my
astonishment, she led me behind the statue, where a secret
gateway lay hidden. It was as if the statue itself was a
concealed entrance. We entered through the gate, and I
found myself in a secret altar room. At its centre stood a
marble statue of a hooded figure a statue I hoped,
surrounded by a pool of water as clear as the sky.
“Go inside the water,” Liza instructed, pushing me
gently towards it. “I will ask you to repeat the words I say,
and remember, if you mess up, it might even be fatal.”
As I stepped into the water, inching forward towards
the creepy statue I braced myself for whatever lay ahead.
The gravity of the situation was apparent, and I knew I
had to focus entirely on following Liza’s instructions with
precision and care.
“But then again, my mind was all about second-
guessing it,” I thought to myself. Before proceeding, Liza
humorously reminded me, “Before that, I almost forgot to
ask your name.” With a smile, I replied, “It’s Jonnathan,
but please call me Jerry.”
“Okay, Jerry, get ready for your enlightenment,
remember to hold the catalyst up in the air” Liza said,
bending down to touch the water. As her hand made
contact, the water began to glow intensely, emitting a
white light. With a commanding tone, Liza instructed me,
69
Teerth Sharma
“Repeat with me now.”
“I, Jonnathan ‘Jerry,’ want to sacrifice my boundless
potential, a portion of my life force, the instigator’s mana
filling the pool, and this catalyst. I also seek the aid of the
first hero’s sacrifice of 900 million to help me gain entry
into the index of magic,” Liza recited.
“I, Jonnathan ‘Jerry,’ want to sacrifice my boundless
potential, a portion of my life force, the instigator’s mana
filling the pool, and this catalyst. I also seek the aid of the
first hero’s sacrifice of 900 million to help me gain entry
into the index of magic,” I echoed her words. As I did, my
body began to glow as brilliantly as the pool I was
immersed in. The aura surrounding me grew more radiant,
I felt my body itself burning. My knees gave away and my
eyes couldn’t open I found myself yet again in darkness.
70
Threshold’s Pursuit

14. Inert Awakening.title: 14. Inert Awakening
14. Inert Awakening.text:
As I regained consciousness, I found myself once
again in the white expanse of nothingness where my
adventure had begun. The same voice that initiated this
journey resonated within and around me, asking, “So, it
seems you have successfully reached a place that supports
your political and social ideals, does it?”
“I believe so,” I replied, trying to sound less
confrontational and more genuinely puzzled, as the last
thing I wanted was to be instantly evaporated.
“I see,” the god responded, his tone filled with
understanding. “As the catalyst of this quest, I am glad to
witness that you have rekindled your will to live.”
His words struck a chord within me, and I began to
contemplate the reason behind my past decision. “Will to
live,” I repeated, finding the phrase foreign to my tongue.
It was then that I realized the true motivation behind my
previous actions. It wasn’t that I despised life or lacked a
desire to live. Instead, it was the feeling of being trapped,
unable to amend my mistakes or change the opinions of
those around me. My only escape was to indulge in this
escapist fantasy, a way to find happiness and solace in a
world of my own making. That’s why I had resorted to
such drastic measures in the past, that’s why I went to the
cell tower, I pondered.
“Oh, before you delve too deeply into the memory lane
and ponder the reasons for your demise, please
understand that my time is limited. I have universes to
create and destroy,” the god interjected with a sense of
71
Teerth Sharma
humility, though I sensed a genuine fondness in his words.
It was the first time someone seemed to appreciate my
company, even if it was merely a facade of benevolence.
“Let me get straight to the point,” he continued. “As a
reward for getting a Kingdom to support and sponsor you,
I shall grant you the power to summon one of the three
people you desired to bring to this world as of our deal.”
His voice brimmed with pride as he bestowed the offer
upon me.
The magnitude of the gift left me speechless. I could
now reunite with Charlie or perhaps invite her to this
captivating realm. Yet, before I could even contemplate
who to summon, I succumbed to unconsciousness once
more.
As I regained consciousness, I found myself no longer
in the presence of the god. Instead, Liza stood before me,
her expression filled with worry. “Jerry, what took you so
long?” she inquired anxiously, but before I could respond,
my vision shifted once again. Just as before, I saw Liza
surrounded by her red aura, and the familiar health and
mana bars. However, this time, a new addition appeared
– a green bar revolving around her head, indicating her
perfect health, and a yellow bar revolving around her
torso, indicating her full mana. Additionally, a
notification-like window appeared before my eyes, much
like the ones you receive on your phone for messages. It
read ‘WITCH OF ANNIHILATION,’ indicating that she
was maintaining her title at the time.
“I can clearly see your health, mana, and title, but
where is my level bar? If this is like a game, then my level
bar should be displayed right in the corner,” I asked Liza
with genuine curiosity, trying to make sense of the
72
Threshold’s Pursuit
situation.
Liza smiled, “For the first time, I could see
determination in your eyes. It’s a nice look to have.
Earlier, you looked as dead as a fish out of water, and that
disgusted me. Also, if you are looking for your level, just
think of ‘level’ in your mind, and you will receive a
notification. Sir First called it ‘The Index,’ and it will
display your level.”
Intrigued, I followed Liza’s advice. First, I activated
the Index of Magic, and then I focused on my own level.
Suddenly, a window appeared before me, glowing with an
outline of yellow with a black interior, similar to the
previous notifications. It displayed ‘Level – 100’ on the
top line and ‘Titles: Dragon Slayer, God’s Agent’ on the
second line, both illuminated in blue. As I read the last
line, I was completely dumbfounded – ‘Species – Divine
Being.’ I realized that from the very beginning, my level
was an astonishing 100, and I wasn’t a human, but a
divine being.
Feeling curious, I decided to put my newly discovered
title to the test. “Activate title: Dragon Slayer,” I said
aloud, and to my surprise, the system promptly heeded my
command.
As I regained my touch of reality once more, I realized
that the system had taken the dragon’s claw from my hand
to use as a catalyst. “So, you have the title of dragon
slayer. Well, it’s an uncommon one for sure, but what
enhancements does it provide?” asked a curious Liza.
“How do I check for that?” I asked, feeling
embarrassed by my lack of knowledge.
“Reactivate your index and think about titles. And
please, if you want to activate another title, do it quietly.
73
Teerth Sharma
The purpose of titles is to surprise your opponent’s when
the power comes into effect, not to announce it
beforehand and give them a chance to dice you into
pieces,” she explained, her tone respectful yet firm, much
like how she treated her students.
“Alright,” I said, taking her advice seriously. I
activated my index once again and focused on the titles.
The same window appeared, this time with a small
paragraph written about the title.
“Title: Dragon Slayer,
Rarity: Uncommon,
Conditions: Slaying a level 200 dragon,
Enhancements: Amplifications of one’s innate
strength by 10 times, invoking fear in lesser mana imbued
beings, faster regeneration,” I read aloud for Liza to hear
and judge.
“Not bad for a rookie,” she winked, and I couldn’t help
but feel a sense of pride. “Well, I am level 100, so don’t
call me a rookie,” I said, trying to flex my level with a hint
of humor.
As Liza laughed loudly, her voice echoing through the
entire hall, she asked me, “Wait, what do you think my
level is?” Still feeling a bit embarrassed, I replied,
“Maybe 100 as well.”
She couldn’t hold her laughter and continued, “Babe,
mine is 1,000,000. The level cap for divine beings is at
100,000,000. Even elven babies are at level 120 at least.
But you may be strong for humans; they go up to level
120 and stop.”
Realizing my mistake, I felt a mixture of awe and
embarrassment. Liza, the great witch, had a level far
beyond my comprehension. I decided to learn more about
74
Threshold’s Pursuit
leveling up from her. “So, how do I level up?” I asked
politely.
“It’s easy,” she explained, regaining her composure.
“You can either give the souls of the enemies you’ve
beaten to the system or gradually feed your mana to it
every day. The first method will level you up faster, but
the second one works too. The best way, though, is to use
magic. You can sacrifice your exhaustion for levels, and
that’s how Sir First laid it out smartly.”
Her words intrigued me, as I realized that even with a
system in place, magic remained the key to gaining
strength in this world. “Liza, will the Witch of
Annihilation teach me magic?” I asked politely. But
before she could answer, we heard loud screams from
outside.
“They are attacking!” Liza exclaimed, rushing out of
the door. I followed her, my heart pounding with a mix of
fear and excitement.
75
Teerth Sharma
15. Dance Of Life?
As Liza and I hurriedly rushed out of the heart of the
cathedral, we spotted a few people running deep into the
corridors, whom I had yet to see before. “Listen, Jerry, I
cannot fight them, and I’m not sure you can take them
down without magic. So, before you rush into battle, I’ll
give you a brief understanding of magic,” Liza said, trying
to keep up with her words as she ran as quickly as her
magnificent dress allowed.
“Magic has two aspects: speed and potency,” she
explained, looking at me as if that alone should be enough
to grasp the entire concept. However, I must have
appeared more dumbfounded than I thought, as we
reached the main gate of the cathedral, and she rolled her
eyes before continuing.
“Imagine you want a heat source. What are your
options? You can have a fire, right? Or perhaps change
the temperature. With magic, you can use mana to rapidly
and efficiently move and shake every particle around you,
but it drains mana in the long run. Another option is to use
hydrogen from the various gases present around you or
even from water, using mana to burn it and create a long-
lasting heat source with just an upfront usage of mana.
Lastly, you can fuse hydrogen atoms together, just like in
the sun, which is highly efficient but requires a lot of
mana. Just like that, magic is all about finding what the
situation requires and what you have in your arsenal. The
rest is basic chemistry and physics, which I’m sure you
must be good at,” Liza explained, giving me a simplified
76
Threshold’s Pursuit
understanding of the complex magic, they used in this
world.
“Last but not least, I can at least offer you this,” said
the Witch of Annihilation. Suddenly, magical shapes
appeared all around her, much like the first time I had met
her when she was using her magic. In an instant, my body
felt a surge of warmth, and a tingling sensation spread
across my abdomen.
“I have infused some natural mana into your skin,
which will strengthen your tissues,” she explained. “I
wish I could do more, but my hands are tied as of my
position.” The sorrow in her expression was genuine, and
it touched me deeply. In my original world, people had
often taken me for granted, and I had grown accustomed
to recognizing disingenuous behaviour. However, the
Witch of Annihilation was different; she was as sincere as
she could be, even feeling sorrow for my fate, despite the
fact that it was my own actions that had led to this war in
her kingdom.
With newfound confidence, I assured her, “Don’t
worry, I am a Hero.” With those words, I boldly opened
the gates of the cathedral, ready to face the chaos.
As I ventured beyond the protective shade of the
strongest witch, my confidence waned. The air was filled
with agonizing shrieks, but it seemed the invaders hadn’t
reached the cathedral. Scanning my surroundings, I
realized I was close to the center of the walled city. With
bravery in my heart, I applied pressure to the ground,
propelling myself forward as fast as I could. The friction
of 0.9 building up on my sneakers – a foolish choice of
footwear for a battle – reminded me of my hasty rush to
confront the war, clad in just a t-shirt, jeans, and sneakers,
77
Teerth Sharma
akin to a true hero, or so I imagined.
Amid my sprint, I encountered many civilians seeking
refuge within their own city. Despite their desperate pleas
for help, I couldn’t afford to halt; stopping would create
an endless cycle of assistance, leaving more people in
similar dire circumstances. The weight of their suffering
clouded my thoughts as I reached the city wall, where I
witnessed black-dressed humans with green hair fighting
individuals who bore an eerie resemblance to the hound I
had met earlier. “This is for Ksir,” one of the fighters
declared as they tore apart the black-dressed humans, they
were clearly the invaders so these green hair people must
be the half-elves I figured.
Determined to aid them, I charged towards the half-
elves. However, my progress was impeded by two
hounds, effectively halting me in my tracks.
Suddenly, the one on my left, who had blond hair for
some reason, emitted the unmistakable scent of a real dog,
sniffing air around. “You smell like a human. Are you the
slave that Ksir was about to trade?” he loudly inquired,
causing some of his comrades to pause upon hearing the
fallen one’s name. “Come with us,” said the person on my
right, who had red hair. “Together, we will bring down
this kingdom. It disgusts me that it even exists in the first
place – a place filled with good-for-nothing people,” the
person on the left declared, revealing his disdain, which
he believed to be a good idea. However, his words felt like
a gut punch to me.
“These good-for-nothing people saved me from being
a slave,” I responded, my anger simmering within.
Typically, I avoided getting angry, often playing the role
of a peacemaker to dissuade others from engaging in
78
Threshold’s Pursuit
confrontations with troublemakers. But not today. The
emotions surged, and without hesitation, I let my fury
speak for itself. In an instant, I threw a powerful punch,
connecting it with the face of the person who had
disgusted me with his words.
As my punch connected, it created a visible
shockwave of air that rippled through the surroundings.
The person I struck was sent flying backward, and the
others looked on in shock before retaliating. Realizing my
own recklessness, I wasn’t prepared to stop being
impulsive just yet. I crouched down and jumped as if
attempting a high jump, only to find myself soaring easily
for at least 20 meters midair. The severity of the situation
became evident as I gained altitude, observing more
hounds approaching the city like a relentless swarm of
ants.
In a swift decision, I knew how to halt my momentum
and landed on a hound, utilizing the pressure of his falling
body to break his bones upon impact. With a bunny jump,
I quickly transitioned to another hound, twisting its neck
before regaining my stance. “So, who else wants to meet
their end?” I asked with a grim smile, eager to explore the
limits of my newfound strength.
I soon realized that these clichés didn’t work in real
life, as the adversaries I challenged stepped forward, each
revealing their unique and deadly gifts. One had a katana
for a wrist, another had nails as long as a katana, while
another sported a gun for a wrist. There were several
others whose transformations I missed as I witnessed a
bullet hurtling towards me with a blistering speed of 1,422
m/s. from that fleeting moment, the physical limitations
of humans was forgotten, as I felt an overwhelming surge
79
Teerth Sharma
of power. With adrenaline coursing through my veins, I
reacted swiftly, plucking the bullet out of mid-air with the
prowess of a seasoned superhero.
Without hesitation, I leaped towards a group of well-
armed militants, lacking any weapon in my hand. Amidst
the chaos, I realized the extent of my impetuousness. My
actions seemed to be driven by an uncontrollable force as
I approached the one with a katana for a hand. My speed
was beyond anything my body had ever experienced
before. With a savage ferocity, I shattered his arm, ripping
off the bones to fashion my own makeshift katana.
“Come, let the dance of life and death begin,” I
bellowed, facing off against groups of two with
complementary abilities. It didn’t matter how well their
abilities aligned; it was inconsequential before my
newfound might. In a blink of an eye, I eliminated almost
everyone standing in front of me, leaving just two hounds
with gun-type abilities. As I faced them, I sensed a
mounting danger. Without warning, 100 different types of
bullets were hurtling towards me with the intent to
obliterate me with a single strike. All I had was one sword.
80
Threshold’s Pursuit

16. Newcomer.title: 16. Newcomer
16. Newcomer.text:
“Not just any sword,” echoed a voice in my mind, and
I suddenly realized that I wasn’t carrying an ordinary
weapon. It was like a katana, with its distinct curved
blade. A realization dawned upon me – katanas were
known to cut through bullets. And even if they couldn’t, I
was swift enough to evade them all. Standing strong and
resolute, I prepared to cut all the bullets in half as soon as
they reached my striking zone. In the blink of an eye, all
the ammunition they had was finished, and I began to
close the distance between us. However, they were no
fools; seeing the lifeless bodies of their comrades, they
knew that I meant to kill them too. Panic struck, and they
started fleeing, but I had had enough of their atrocities.
Taking a deep breath, I focused my mind. It was time
to employ a technique they didn’t possess. “If Liza said I
can move particles around, I might as well make them still
and create a wall,” I whispered to myself, concentrating
on the task at hand while running toward them. Suddenly,
knowledge flooded my mind, as if the ability to
manipulate the world around me had always been there. I
understood the x, y, z vectors and the required mana for
the task, along with the rate of consumption per minute.
It was as if I had been given a map of the universe’s
secrets. “I don’t need it for long,” I reassured myself,
channelling all my strength into my legs and dashing
forward. My rivals took off in a desperate attempt to
escape, but they were brought to an abrupt halt by the
invisible wall I had created.
81
Teerth Sharma
Seeing my chance, I closed the distance to my
dumbfounded foes. With a swift attack, I sliced through
one of them, and with my left hand, I crushed their skull,
putting an end to their futile attempts to escape.
As I surveyed the aftermath, I witnessed the carnage
that my bloodlust had wrought. The lifeless bodies of
those half-elves, who sacrificed themselves due to my
own pathetic actions, lay strewn around me. My heart
ached with grief as I caught sight of the last surviving
hound the criminals, trembling in fear on the ground. “So,
you are the sole witness to my butchery acts,” I declared
with a mixture of confidence and remorse, the soft rays of
dawn illuminating my troubled face.
As the sun broke through the sky, casting a gentle glow
on the scene, I approached the weeping hound. He
appeared to be around my age, with dark brown hair and
striking red irises. Through his tears, he uttered, “Every
herd has a shepherd to guide them, every pack has an
alpha.” His words seemed somewhat muddled, but I paid
them little mind as I moved swiftly towards him, my blade
creating a hazy blur in the air. In one swift motion, I
brought the blade down, ending his suffering.
As I stood over the fallen hound, a new voice suddenly
pierced the silence with a tone of pride. I turned to see a
man dressed in a black Sunday suit with matching shoes,
approaching confidently from the direction of the rising
sun, his shadow elongating as he drew near. He gazed at
the bodies of his comrades, his eyes widening with shock
and grief. “He was right about the shepherd thing, you
know,” he remarked, trying to maintain his composure
despite the grisly sight before him.
A sense of grim satisfaction washed over me as I
82
Threshold’s Pursuit
looked at this man. “I will spare your life, but you must
run, coward,” I taunted, a sinister smile playing on my
lips.
“Let’s see who will need to run in just five seconds,”
the newcomer proclaimed with a hint of amusement,
cracking his fingers one by one and snapping his neck in
a classic standoff cliché. I was prepared for his attack; I
could create an invisible barrier to protect myself. But
then, in the blink of an eye, he blurred into a haze and
reappeared right in front of me. Thankfully, my makeshift
katana allowed me to intercept his deadly punch,
preventing my untimely demise but broke my katana. He
vanished again, moving with an almost teleportation-like
speed.
“So, your gift is teleportation?” I shouted, quickly
forming a defensive wall around me, hoping to counter
his teleporting manoeuvres. But it was futile; he
effortlessly teleported behind me, proving that it wasn’t
teleportation at all. Grabbing my shoulder
“Teleportation? That was merely my speed. Oh, too fast
for you? Want me to slow down?” he taunted with a
condescending voice, clearly enjoying our encounter.
Then he disappeared
Frustration boiled within me as he acted smug, treating
me like a broken object. My heart raced as I mustered all
my mana to confront him head-on. Suddenly, my vision
shifted to a system view mode, displaying my health,
mana, and allowing me to see him as well. He circled
around me like a wolf, calculating his next move. With
determination, I etched a wall of particles from the
direction he was approaching, but his face remained
unchanged. Boredom etched on his sharp chin; he
83
Teerth Sharma
scrutinized me as if I were a specimen in an experiment.
His crimson eyes exuded a sense of uninterested
superiority, while his high-held nose gave him an air of
arrogance. All of this only fuelled my anger further, and I
fortified my mana barrier, ready to make this arrogant brat
suffer.
But, once again, he vanished before my eyes, leaving
me frustrated and on edge.
I looked behind, certain he was resorting to
underhanded tactics to gain the upper hand. But to my
surprise, he appeared from the front, casually punching
the invisible wall I had conjured. To my horror, the space
itself shattered like a broken mirror, creating intricate
cracks that rippled through the air. It seemed as if the very
fabric of reality was momentarily broken before restoring
itself, leaving me astounded by the raw power he
possessed.
Before I could recover from this awe-inspiring display,
the newcomer swiftly appeared in front of me,
disregarding my multiple particle walls. “What are you
using your mana for? Stop wasting it,” he taunted, and in
an instant, his fist slammed into my gut, sending me
hurtling backward. The force of the punch was
overwhelming; it shattered everything, even my spine. I
crumpled to the ground, darkness closing in on me.
Yet, he was not finished. Approaching me, he stood
triumphantly over my fallen form, asserting his
dominance.
My vision shifted to a system view, and I could see my
health plummet to a mere 20%. With death seemingly
imminent, I mustered all the strength I had left and
remembered my last resort - the “Judgment of God”
84
Threshold’s Pursuit
tactic. As I cried out the words, an extraordinary
transformation began to take place. The ground trembled,
and even the sun’s light seemed to fade, as if nature itself
acknowledged the gravity of my plea.
Lying on my deathbed, I glimpsed the dead bodies of
the hounds and elves stirring, a strange energy animating
them. I could feel my mana draining rapidly, but before I
could fully comprehend what was happening, the
newcomer hound kicked me in the face, expressing
irritation at my actions. “What the hell are you doing” he
said However, it was too late to stop what was unfolding.
In my vision, the lifeless bodies of the fallen hounds
and elves rose to their feet, standing tall with an eerie
unity. They stood at attention, as if they were waiting for
orders. It was then that I realized - they were my soldiers
now, both half-elves and hounds, resurrected and under
my command. “Defend me!” I shouted, invoking my
newfound authority over these reanimated warriors.
85
Teerth Sharma

17. Patron.title: 17. Patron
17. Patron.text:
As soon as those words left my mouth, I turned to
observe the newcomer’s reaction. What I saw was beyond
anything I could have imagined. The glint of arrogance he
had carried until now was replaced by a level of disgust
and fury I had never witnessed. His face turned an
alarming shade of red, and his sclera, which was once
white, momentarily got infused with crimson, as if blood
vessels were about to burst. He lunged at me with a punch,
but the shock of my command had slowed him down just
enough for me to react. I managed to hold his fist before
it landed, buying myself a precious moment.
Meanwhile, the resurrected bodies of my fallen
comrades inched forward, bound by my commands, and
the sight seemed to infuriate him even more. I did not
fully understand what was happening, but it was clear that
I was gaining an upper hand against this seemingly
superior opponent, and the thrill of it surged through my
veins. To witness the high and mighty crumble before me
gave me a strange satisfaction.
Despite his anger, he once again disappeared with
lightning speed. I braced myself for his inevitable
reappearance, but this time, he did something unexpected.
He materialized behind me with such velocity that I had
no time to react. I thought my life was about to end, but
instead, he focused his attack on the dead soldiers I had
commanded. With inhuman force, he ripped them apart,
reducing their numbers from around twenty to just one in
a matter of seconds.
86
Threshold’s Pursuit
He then grabbed the last remaining functional
reanimated soldier of his unit, the one with katana for
nails, and threw him at me with a vicious force. The
lifeless body knocked me to the ground, and I struggled
to regain my bearings. The newcomer’s chilling voice
echoed in my ears, taunting me. “You stay dead until I
finish your ace in the hole, and then I will tear you apart
limb by limb.”
As he continued his merciless onslaught, the
realization of how vast the gap between us in terms of
power and speed was dawned on me. It was like facing a
supernatural force, a being so terrifyingly fast and strong
that he could shatter bones and destroy lives with casual
ease. The fear of this super-speedy adversary sent shivers
down my spine, and I knew I had to find a way to turn the
tables before it was too late.
Then I realized what I had at my disposal - the
“Weapon-God” skill, a gift bestowed upon me by the
angel. It granted me tremendous strength when wielding
a katana. Although my makeshift katana had been broken,
I swiftly produced a plan. “I will just make another
katana,” I whispered, seizing the nail from the dead guy
thrown at me. As the newcomer made quick work of the
reanimated half-elves, I tore a piece of my own t-shirt and
fashioned a makeshift katana.
“I actually shall give you credit; I haven’t had this
much fun since forever,” said the newcomer casually, as
he nonchalantly removed the head of a reanimated soldier.
“My name is Deputy Captain Nikvlo ‘Ripper’ Kashikov,
second in command of the Hound unit, which you just
eradicated. So, tell me, oh brave soldier, what might be
your name and who are you associated with?” he inquired.
87
Teerth Sharma
“I am a Hero summoned to this world by the gods to
correct your wrongdoings. If you halt right there and pray,
I might ask the god to forgive your sins,” I replied with a
sense of superiority, raising my newly created katana in
the air.
“Oh, I think you have mistaken me for someone from
the Holy Kingdom, and I don’t buy the idea that you’re a
hero,” retorted Deputy Captain Nikvlo. Suddenly, he
appeared as a hazy figure in the air, and I instinctively
took a fighting stance. The angelic boost I received
allowed me to keep up with his movements, but I knew it
was only possible because of my newfound abilities. It
was time to prove my courage and worthiness of these
powers.
As he materialized in front of me and launched a
punch, I swiftly bent to the side, dodging his attack with
agility. Seizing the opportunity, I struck back with my
katana, but before my blade could connect, he blurred into
a haze and disappeared once again. “You seem to be
getting stronger every second, but you can’t defeat me,”
taunted the deputy with a smug expression.
“And why is that?” I challenged.
“My gift is that my physical prowess doubles every
time I use it. So, every time I use my legs for speed, I am
doubling my speed. Right now, I am a living proof of
breaking what this kingdom calls physics,” he explained
with a smirk.
The repercussions of this knowledge became clear to
me. I could not predict his movements as he blurred into
a haze, leaving me unable to even perceive his aura in my
system viewer mode. Suddenly, he reappeared in front of
me at breakneck speed. I could see him, but my reactions
88
Threshold’s Pursuit
were too slow. His punch connected with my chest, and I
was sent flying backward, gasping for breath as I
struggled to regain my composure. The fight was far from
over, and I needed to find a way to outsmart this
formidable adversary.
As my health bar neared 10%, I realized that even
though I felt mentally fine due to the skill granted by the
angel, my physical condition was far from optimal.
However, I refused to back down. Surprisingly, my mana
bar still appeared filled to the brim, despite the sudden
necromancy I had performed. I had a plan.
As Deputy Captain Nikvlo circled around me like a
wolf preparing for his final strike, I taunted him, “Why
don’t you face someone of your own category? Why hide
behind politics to avoid the wrath of the Witch of
Annihilation?” My words seemed to momentarily
diminish his smug attitude.
“I am not scared of that vampire witch. I am ready to
face her right now,” he retorted, trying to regain his pride.
The trap was set, and I pressed on, suggesting “attack the
Mage Association. It was the last thing you had to worry
about is losing, and if things went south, you could blame
one of the dead soldiers, claiming self-defense during an
official investigation.”
“Yes, that’s a fine idea,” he agreed, but then suspicion
flickered in his eyes. “Are you trying to provoke me?” he
questioned, catching on to my tactics.
“No, I was just curious,” I lied, trying to play innocent.
He did not fall for it. “First, I will kill you, and then
she will join you in hell,” he threatened, turning into a
hazy blur as he prepared to strike.
As he approached me, I whispered, “Activate
89
Teerth Sharma
summoning,” gathering all the mana I had left and using
it to create a hole between worlds. I did not have anyone
particular from my world in mind; I just sought a powerful
ally, someone who could become the protector this
kingdom desperately needed. But before the summoning
could be completed, something extraordinary happened.
As the hound deputy appeared Infront of me about to
sucker punch my face
Time itself seemed to slow, just like it had with the
other hound. My system viewer activated, and a
notification window appeared, the grandest size possible,
covering my entire vision. The title “Judgment of God”
flashed on the screen, and I focused on the buffs it
provided. I was overwhelmed but determined. My mana
regeneration was now 100% of current capacity every ten
seconds, and I had the ability to control the judgment of
gods with a 20% boost to physical strength. Though the
second buff confused me, I pushed it aside, knowing that
my powers were far beyond the ordinary. I was about to
get an ego about it soon I felt.
Yet, I soon realized that time had not actually slowed
down; rather, my body had accelerated so rapidly that
every moving second now felt like a year. This newfound
control was both thrilling and daunting.
“CANCEL SUMMONING!” I shouted, realizing I did
not want to waste this precious resource on someone like
him when I could take him down myself now with this
newfound strength.
Then, I heard a familiar voice, angelic and comforting.
My patron angel floated above me, “I have came here
through the hole in worlds you created for summoning I
was here to see how my brother is working” he said with
90
Threshold’s Pursuit
a casual tone “really thanks for stopping time do that
often” I smiled and said “I can’t stop time for you
anymore,” he said, “the bridge is already weak I can’t
burden it to carry out my will in this world or my powers
will break it remember I am a arch angel and I already
saved you 2 times now “ he added
With newfound determination and a sense of
responsibility, I faced the formidable deputy, but before
my patron’s magic run out, I sucker punched the deputy
with all the might I had “cheap” laughed my patron before
disappearing I readied myself for facing the deputy ripper
one more time with fresh outlook
91
Teerth Sharma

18. Embers Of Triumph.title: 18. Embers Of Triumph
18. Embers Of Triumph.text:
The end of the time stop left me disoriented, as the
effects of the sucker punch reverberated through his
senses. The deputy, who had been accustomed to
managing situations with unparalleled speed, was taken
aback. Even his smug grin could not conceal his
astonishment. He swiftly changed his trajectory and
vanished into a hazy mist, only to reappear circling
around me, resembling the wolf he was.
“Good puppy, now stay away!” I shouted, a moment
of boldness flashing across my face. His circling
intensified, like a predator stalking its prey. He spoke,
voice dripping with a sly edge, “Let’s suppose I take you
up on your theory.”
“What theory?” I inquired, but it was already too late.
He blurred into view right in front of me, an attack
imminent. Swiftly, I dodged his assault, narrowly evading
his strike. However, before I could launch a counterattack,
he hazed back, leaving me off balance.
“The theory of you being a Hero sent by God,” he
stated, returning to his smug demeanour. I readied myself,
waiting for him to approach again for another strike. As
he circled, the tension between us grew.
“Well, that’s not a theory,” I responded, bracing for his
next move. His presence was like a storm, unpredictable
and relentless. He continued his verbal assault, his words
cutting through the charged air. “The First Hero,
renowned as the strongest in the universe, was summoned
here because he was the perfect vessel for the chained
92
Threshold’s Pursuit
Archangel of Destruction. The only one across the
multiverse whose hunger for destruction surpassed even
that of the angel. The Second Hero’s intellect exceeded
anyone compatible with the Angel of Wisdom, granting
him near-omniscient powers. The Third Hero was brought
due to her ability to empathize with and manage humans,
her affinity for the Angel of Healing unmatched. And then
there is our Fourth, summoned as the perfect vessel for
God’s right hand, the hand of creation, a direct rival to the
First Hero, who channels the left hand of God—the hand
of destruction. Against this backdrop, what do you bring
to the table?” he taunted, words laced with a cruel truth.
He continued his circling, calculating, waiting for an
opening. His speech, though meant to undermine me, had
shaken my self-confidence. “That’s right, you are
pathetic,” he proclaimed, his voice resonating with
mockery before vanishing once again in his haze of speed.
He reappeared in my field of vision, but I was a
fraction too slow in reacting. His hand connected with my
arm bone, shattering it instantly on impact, causing me to
release a howl of agony. His speed was truly unparalleled,
and in his bloodlust, he seemed to forget that I was not
just some ordinary soldier. I was the one who had
annihilated his entire squadron of soldiers. Fuelled by this
realization, I devised a simple yet effective strategy.
Waiting for the opportune moment, I bided my time as
he retreated, anticipating my retaliatory strike. With a
quick surge of mana, I enveloped myself, deftly
manipulating the matter around me using the power of
mana. My aim was to disrupt ionic and covalent bonds,
mirroring the behaviour of the atmosphere in my own
world. I harnessed the surroundings, specifically
93
Teerth Sharma
collecting hydrogen, to create a protective barrier. I was
willing to sacrifice myself if necessary to eliminate this
smug adversary once and for all.
But before I could implement this drastic measure, he
hazed right in front of me. His punch landed squarely on
my chest, sending me hurtling backward. It seemed my
hypothesis about hydrogen’s potential combustion with
friction was incorrect, or rather, he transcended the
limitations of friction altogether. My high spirits shattered
in the face of this revelation, and I found myself mentally
preparing for the inevitable end. It felt as though I was on
the brink of death, engulfed in the darkest despair.
However, as the saying goes, it is always darkest
before the dawn.
Depleted of mana, I channelled all my energy into
wielding my sword with precision and skill, hoping it
would be my best defence against the lightning-fast
opponent before me. In the midst of our high-speed duel,
a spark of inspiration ignited within me. I remembered the
unique property of mana, how it could forcibly
manipulate the very nature of particles in the surrounding
environment.
As he charged toward me with lethal intent, I held my
ground, waiting until the last possible moment. Just as he
materialized in front of me, his fist aimed to strike, I
executed a swift dodge, narrowly avoiding the blow.
Seizing the opportunity, I swung my katana with
calculated force, aiming to cut through his form. I knew it
was a long shot, but this move was never meant to land a
hit—it was a strategic manoeuvre to create some distance
between us.
True to my expectations, he retreated, his agile figure
94
Threshold’s Pursuit
resuming its predatory circles around me. I anticipated
this pattern and bided my time, waiting for the perfect
moment. When he encroached upon the area I had
manipulated, where the density of hydrogen particles had
been subtly increased, I took advantage of the recently
regenerated mana granted by my newly acquired title,
“Judgment of God.”
Harnessing the power of the ‘all-penetrating’ mana, I
conjured electricity within the manipulated hydrogen
field. To my astonishment, my impromptu plan worked
flawlessly. A surge of electric energy coursed through the
hydrogen-enriched air, and in a spectacular display, a
dazzling blaze erupted around the hound deputy. The air
crackled with energy, and the flames danced like vengeful
spirits, cloaking him in a fierce conflagration.
The sight was both mesmerizing and triumphant, a
moment relief flooded my body as I view the aftermath.
The aftermath of our intense battle, made a sense of
exhilaration wash over me. It took several seconds for my
consciousness to settle, and summoning my courage, I
approached the charred remains of the deputy. The sight
of his charred body added to my confidence; it seemed
that my plan had indeed worked. The area around him,
once an old stone road, was now marred by cracks. He lay
there, surrounded by his fallen comrades, as if death had
shown him mercy. Even in death, there was a serene smile
on his face. This smile didn’t appear smug; rather, it
seemed to reflect a genuine moment of peace as he
reunited with an old colleague — death. They had likely
worked together for decades, and now, in his passing, he
looked content. This sight reminded me of my own past
suicide attempt, when I wasn’t embracing death with
95
Teerth Sharma
happiness. It made me realize the significance of a
fulfilling death. One should depart this world with a sense
of satisfaction, not regret. I found myself thankful to the
deputy for imparting this valuable lesson.
As I pondered the profound meaning of a meaningful
death, my thoughts were interrupted by the presence of an
unexpected but intriguing ally. The Witch of
Annihilation, Liza, had arrived to meet me. It appeared
she had been observing the entirety of our battle from a
distance. Her voice carried a mix of appreciation and
seriousness as she addressed me, “I must commend your
valiant fight. Your efforts won’t go unnoticed. However,
what transpired here today cannot be ignored.” Her face
held a determined expression, yet her eyes retained their
playful glint.
96
Threshold’s Pursuit

19. Shadows Of Authority.title: 19. Shadows Of Authority
19. Shadows Of Authority.text:
(Charlie’s POV)
The world itself appears to me as a labyrinth of
dreams, a realm where we chase after our desires with
fervour, sometimes even sacrificing everything for their
pursuit. Yet, ironically, even after knowing about the
labyrinth I find myself ensnared within this very web of
dreams. The more I strive to break free, the deeper I seem
to sink into its clutches. It’s as if my attempts to escape
merely loop back into endless dreams, leaving me trapped
in a paradoxical cycle. I reflect on this intricate matrix of
dreams that both guides and imprisons our lives.
There was a time, undoubtedly, when I wasn’t so
entwined in this dream-like existence. The possibility of
me being the same person I am now would have been
drastically altered, perhaps even extinguished. In that
alternate reality, a different version of me would have
been present, maybe not as accomplished as my current
self, but almost certainly happier.
The thought tickles my imagination—another version
of myself, possibly leading a life filled with more
contentment.
It’s clear that discussing my potential contentment
wouldn’t resonate with the world around me, a world
where material wealth often takes centre stage in the
pursuit of happiness. In the realm of mortals, the all-
mighty dollar seems to hold dominion, and the relentless
quest for it is often equated with a journey toward a divine
purpose. It’s as though, in the hierarchy of priorities, the
97
Teerth Sharma
pursuit of wealth resides just slightly above the sacred.
Interestingly, despite my accumulated wisdom, I
didn’t choose the path of renunciation, opting to embrace
a life of simplicity like a monk might. No, instead, I
accumulated riches and possessions, chasing after what
the world deemed valuable. Yet, the irony couldn’t be
starker—I find myself still grappling with dissatisfaction.
Amidst the luxurious possessions, the overflowing
wealth, there remains an emptiness that defies
explanation.
While I hesitate to label the almighty dollar as useless,
it undeniably serves as the catalyst for lives lived on the
edge of exhilaration and the promise of adventure. It
propels individuals out of their comfort zones, urging
them to traverse uncharted territories in the pursuit of
greater rewards. This dynamic sustains a portion of the
corporate world, fueling the vitality of what we
sometimes term “corporate rats.”
However, what I yearn for isn’t just the dismissal of
the dollar’s significance. Instead, I crave the return to a
life of raw adventure, reminiscent of a place where
currency held no power and skill was the compass guiding
our endeavors. Those were the moments when life wasn’t
constrained by material wealth but was measured by the
mastery of our abilities. And yet, even amidst such
unfettered existence, I came to a resounding realization —
I would never satiate my desires. My thirst for more, my
craving to excel, seemed insatiable, perhaps a
manifestation of the sin of wrath that lingered within me.
Not envy, not gluttony, but wrath — a unique kind that
didn’t breed hatred for others’ achievements, but a
seething resentment towards my own inadequacies. This
98
Threshold’s Pursuit
relentless thirst for power, an unquenchable desire, stood
like an impenetrable barrier between me and true
happiness.
As I typed away, I found myself seated in the luxurious
confines of my beloved Cadillac Limousine. This very
vehicle, often dubbed by the media as the “haven of
opulence amidst the chaos of the outside world,” had
witnessed numerous encounters with powerful figures.
Yet, on this particular day, it was an unremarkable
backdrop as I embarked on a sombre journey to attend a
funeral.
The destination held memories that clashed with the
extravagant nature of the car. Today was not a day to
reminisce about opulence, but to commemorate a
cherished friend. He had never seen me merely as a
wealthy acquaintance; instead, he valued my wisdom. It
wasn’t my riches that mattered to him, but the depth of
understanding that came with them. Ironically, his values
were far removed from the materialistic pursuits that often
consumed me.
And now, here I was, grappling with the devastating
reality that he had taken his own life. A heinous act that
left me stunned and bewildered. I questioned why he
hadn’t turned to me, his wealth-flaunting friend, for
anything he desired. But it was evident that his struggles
were beyond mere desires; they delved into the realm of
love, a force I had never been adept at comprehending, let
alone reasoning with. For even I had my encounters with
it, and they were never pleasant.
Regret gnawed at me, a heavy burden to bear. In a
world where my presence held the sway to procure
anything, my absence at the time he needed me the most
99
Teerth Sharma
left a gaping wound. As I continued to drive, the weight
of this regret only grew heavier.
As I arrived at the funeral site, a surge of anger ignited
within me. The scene before me seemed to fuel the fire
within my veins. My dear friend lay in a casket crafted
from the purest oak, a vessel for his departed soul.
However, as I drew closer, paying no heed to the somber
crowd that had gathered, my heart sank even further. The
expression on his lifeless face revealed a truth that cut to
the core—he hadn’t known happiness in his lifetime. It
was as if he had been so convinced that joy was beyond
his reach that he had chosen the path that led us to this
mournful gathering.
Pushing aside the throng of onlookers, I focused solely
on him. His face told a story of unspoken pain, of a life
that had been devoid of the happiness he so deserved. It
was a heart-wrenching realization that he had felt so
certain of his perpetual sorrow that he had chosen this
tragic end.
As I paid my final respects to my fallen friend, my
grief overwhelmed me. Unable to hold back the torrent of
emotions, I found myself sobbing uncontrollably over his
oakwood coffin. In my life, I had weathered the storms of
people’s cruelty, never letting it affect me. But Jerry, my
dear friend, had borne the weight of such torment. He
should have spoken up, he should have reached out, for I
would have moved mountains to ease his suffering. I
would have unleashed assassins or even taken lives
myself if he so wished. Why had he remained silent?
What had given him the right to endure his struggles in
solitude?
Anguish gripped my heart as I lamented the promise
100
Threshold’s Pursuit
we had once made—to share each other’s burdens. How
dare he leave this world before me? The weight of his
absence was crushing, and my tears fell onto the coffin as
a testament to the depth of my sorrow.
As I stepped away from the center stage, allowing the
focus to rightfully shift to my departed friend, I caught
sight of a face that ignited a blaze of anger within me. It
was the very same face that had held Jerry’s heart, the
reason for his untimely journey into eternity. It was
‘her’—the girl Jerry had adored, the one whose presence
had prompted his fateful path to heaven. And the only
person in this whole wide world that should have avoided
this ceremony. My instinct was to rush forward, fueled by
a surge of rage, and deliver a punch with all the force I
could muster. But as she turned to face me, my hand froze
mid-air.
Confronted by her tear-streaked face, my anger
faltered. She wasn’t here merely to look at the part; her
tears were genuine. The girl, who had been the cause of
so much turmoil, stood before me, her sorrow evident. It
was the same sorrow I had felt when my wrath took
control. “I thought you never loved him, so why are you
crying here? Just a ploy to gain my sympathy, you hag,”
I challenged, my words laced with bitterness. I felt a
verbal duel was the least she deserved, given her role as
the catalyst.
However, as her voice met my ears, my resolve
wavered. Her response held a mix of guilt and sadness, a
tone that I recognized all too well. It was the voice I had
often used—riddled with remorse and longing. “Charlie,
you of all people know why I couldn’t be with Jerry. And
no, I’m not seeking favors from you by shedding tears.
101
Teerth Sharma
Despite not being his girlfriend, I was his close friend. He
was there for me in times of need, even when I couldn’t
pinpoint the source of my sadness. He offered unwavering
support, even when I was unable to articulate my
struggles. So, you don’t have the right to tarnish my last
memory of him just because you couldn’t control your
wrath,” she spoke, her words carrying the weight of both
guilt and sorrow.
Stunned by her response, my hand fell to my side, and
I retreated, unable to muster a response. Instead, I
retreated from the situation, my thoughts in turmoil. I
moved through the crowd, keeping my words unspoken,
until I reached Jerry’s grieving parents. There, I engaged
in conversation, offering whatever support I could
provide. I made it my mission to ensure that Jerry’s
siblings would find happiness, a lasting tribute to his
memory.
The funeral unfolded against a backdrop of sadness
and contemplation. Emotions ran deep, the weight of our
loss hanging in the air, tugging at the heartstrings of all
present. The sight of Jerry’s lifeless form in the casket was
a painful reminder of the fragility of life. Each tear shed
was a testament to the bonds we shared, the unspoken
words and the moments forever lost to time.
As I managed to slip away during the prayers, a man
in a sleek black suit crossed my path. His demeanour
suggested authority, as if he were about to read a will. Just
as I moved to distance myself, he stepped closer, his
presence impossible to ignore. “Sir,” he began, his tone
both formal and urgent, “you have been summoned by the
Observationis Fundamenta and If I may be so bold, I
implore you to lower your magical pressure, lest this
102
Threshold’s Pursuit
sacred church crumble under its power.”
A mysterious voice echoed the man’s warning,
emphasizing the gravity of the situation. “Peregrino
Observationis Fundamenta wants me now?” I inquired, a
mix of curiosity and exasperation lacing my words. The
timing felt inappropriate for political games or the
introduction of new policies. All I yearned for was to be
left in peace, to reflect on my dear friend Jerry and to
ensure that no one else suffered as he had. But the voice’s
presence vanished as quickly as it had appeared. It
dawned on me that the world government’s concern for
Jerry’s well-being had likely been non-existent. This was
no moment to ignite a rebellion against the world; it was
a time to grieve.
Reluctantly, I surrendered to the embrace of my
waiting Cadillac, instructing the driver to head for the
nearest stronghold. The leather seats cocooned me in a
sense of isolation as the vehicle glided forward. The
outside world blurred by a backdrop to my sombre
thoughts. My heart ached for the friend I had lost and for
the journey that lay ahead.
103
Teerth Sharma

20. Resonance Of Fear.title: 20. Resonance Of Fear
20. Resonance Of Fear.text:
“Bit of a cloudy day, isn’t it?” I ventured, injecting a
touch of conversation into the heavy silence that draped
around us like a wet blanket. The sky above was a tapestry
of Gray, a reflection of the mood that enveloped me.
“Indeed, sir,” the driver replied in that impeccably
British way of his. His voice held the essence of a refined
gentleman, a perfect complement to the sophisticated
chaos of the city.
“But if I’m not mistaken, the stronghold should be just
a stone’s throw away, shouldn’t it?” I pressed on, a feeble
attempt to engage in dialogue that might distract me from
my thoughts.
“Absolutely spot-on, sir,” he confirmed, his response
laced with an air of empathy. He seemed to understand
the weight of my emotions, the heaviness that came with
the loss of a friend. His willingness to engage in the
conversation was a soothing balm for my troubled mind.
Yet, the notion of comfort eluded me. The blame for
my friend’s tragic end rested squarely on my shoulders, a
reality I couldn’t escape. The tendrils of karma wound
around my existence, weaving a complex tapestry of
challenges and hardships for both me and those around
me. Every twist and turn seemed to lead to another
challenge, creating an endless loop of trials that refused to
relent. In the midst of this journey to the stronghold, I
realized that I still had my diary to maintain. And so,
within the quiet confines of the backseat, I began to pen
down my thoughts, finding a semblance of solace amid
104
Threshold’s Pursuit
the turmoil.
“Sir, if I may venture a question, why might the
government be summoning you at such a delicate
juncture?” The driver’s voice cut through the silence, his
words carrying a gentle sympathy as he deftly maneuverer
through the congested traffic.
Ah, the government—the orchestrator of policies and
power plays. “Well,” I began with a blend of cynicism and
weariness, “the government, you see, has a peculiar
affinity for ideologies and securing voter loyalty.
Individuals hardly register on their radar. Swap those
voters for voting birds, and I doubt they’d lose sleep.
They’ve beckoned me because they require something
from me, something so crucial that even the media’s glare
won’t dissuade them. I’m half-expecting they’ll ask for
more charitable contributions. Not that I’m averse to
giving, mind you, but when you’ve already doled out a
hefty 4.5 billion, even the greediest among them should
be appeased.” My words held a tinge of frustration, a
tangle of emotions that I allowed to unravel. There was no
chance the driver would offer a contrary opinion; his
loyalty lay steadfast.
“Absolutely, sir. It’s quite the education, gaining
insights into these matters while in your esteemed
company,” the driver responded, his gaze firmly fixed on
the road ahead, his British smile as unwavering as his
focus.
“Ah, you, hailing from the land of Her Majesty,
wouldn’t be steeped in such political nuances,” I sighed,
feeling the weight of years press upon me.
“Here we are, sir,” the driver’s voice pulled me from
my thoughts as we pulled up in front of a modest five-
105
Teerth Sharma
story building. Its unassuming facade was far from what
one might expect of a government establishment, but then
again, this building didn’t belong to any one government.
It was more accurate to say that every government
belonged to the enigmatic group that owned this
unmarked structure.
I stepped out of the Cadillac, my gaze sweeping over
the building’s exterior. “Tinted bulletproof glass and
titanium alloy doors,” the driver announced, his voice
carrying a note of amusement, “Looks like they’re our
clientele.” He held the door open for me, and I couldn’t
help but smile at his assessment. He had a knack for
spotting the finer details that often went unnoticed.
